<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Active and Inactive Allagan Tomeliths■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
All Allagan tomeliths are inactive at the start of the campaign, and will activate randomly at set intervals. Each tomelith has a limited quantity of data that can be retrieved, and will become inactive once that limit is reached. As the campaign progresses, the maximum number of simultaneously active Allagan tomeliths will change.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Occupying Allagan Tomeliths■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Active Allagan tomeliths will be either “Unoccupied” or “Occupied” depending on whether or not a Grand Company has occupied them. If a player occupies an unoccupied tomelith, its status will change from Unoccupied to Occupied. If the tomelith is already occupied by an opposing faction, accessing the tomelith will change its status to Unoccupied. Tomeliths are Unoccupied when they change from inactive to active. Attempts to occupy a tomelith will be interrupted if the player is attacked.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Allagan Tomelith Ranks■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Allagan tomeliths will activate at one of three ranks: B, A, or S. Although tomeliths have a lower chance to activate at higher ranks, they hold a larger quantity of data that can be retrieved while they are occupied. As the match progresses, tomeliths will have an increased chance to activate at higher ranks.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Changes According to Time Remaining■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
As time passes, the maximum number of tomeliths that can be active simultaneously decreases. However, it becomes easier to activate higher-ranked tomeliths.

**■Active and Inactive Allagan Tomeliths■**
All Allagan tomeliths are inactive at the start of the campaign, and will activate randomly at set intervals. Each tomelith has a limited quantity of data that can be retrieved, and will become inactive once that limit is reached. As the campaign progresses, the maximum number of simultaneously active Allagan tomeliths will change.

**■Occupying Allagan Tomeliths■**
Active Allagan tomeliths will be either “Unoccupied” or “Occupied” depending on whether or not a Grand Company has occupied them. If a player occupies an unoccupied tomelith, its status will change from Unoccupied to Occupied. If the tomelith is already occupied by an opposing faction, accessing the tomelith will change its status to Unoccupied. Tomeliths are Unoccupied when they change from inactive to active. Attempts to occupy a tomelith will be interrupted if the player is attacked.

**■Allagan Tomelith Ranks■**
Allagan tomeliths will activate at one of three ranks: B, A, or S. Although tomeliths have a lower chance to activate at higher ranks, they hold a larger quantity of data that can be retrieved while they are occupied. As the match progresses, tomeliths will have an increased chance to activate at higher ranks.

**■Changes According to Time Remaining■**
As time passes, the maximum number of tomeliths that can be active simultaneously decreases. However, it becomes easier to activate higher-ranked tomeliths.

Under the influence of the lesser moon Dalamud, Nael van Darnus, legatus of the VIIth Legion, sought out and unearthed Allagan ruins in the eastern lowlands of Coerthas. Aided by the knowledge contained therein, he raised the very earth unto the heavens, and from this lofty perch did the White Raven nearly orchestrate the destruction of Eorzea.

The remnants of that mad plan still litter the frozen lowlands, as well as other ancient artifacts now laid bare. Though the powers that be are in agreement that Nael's data must be secured, they remain divided on the subject of how best to administer the region, which has prompted them to invoke the same laws which transformed Carteneau and Seal Rock into a battlefield. Where words fail, weapons speak.

In the event that none of the three alliances accumulates the required amount of data within the time limit, victory will be awarded to the alliance with the most data.

The data from icebound tomeliths can be collected by attacking them, and once they are destroyed, data will be rewarded to each Grand Company based on the amount of damage inflicted.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Activating Icebound Tomeliths■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Icebound tomeliths can only be destroyed once they have become active. They will be inactive at the start of the campaign, and will activate at random after a short period of time. Any icebound tomeliths destroyed during the course of the campaign will regenerate in an inactive state after a fixed amount of time.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Icebound Tomelith■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Active icebound tomeliths will be either large or small, with large tomeliths containing more data.

The data from icebound tomeliths can be collected by attacking them, and once they are destroyed, data will be rewarded to each Grand Company based on the amount of damage inflicted.

Icebound tomeliths can only be destroyed once they have become active. They will be inactive at the start of the campaign, and will activate at random after a short period of time. Any icebound tomeliths destroyed during the course of the campaign will regenerate in an inactive state after a fixed amount of time.

**■Icebound Tomelith■**
Active icebound tomeliths will be either large or small, with large tomeliths containing more data.

In the event that none of the three alliances reaches the specified tactical rating within the time limit, victory will be awarded to the alliance with the highest tactical rating.

Across the field of battle, there are sacred sites known as ovoos. By laying claim to these locations, players can increase their team's tactical rating over time.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Ovoo States■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
In the course of battle, an ovoo will assume one of four states, in the following order:

1. Inactive
While inactive, an ovoo cannot be claimed. All ovoos are inactive at the onset of battle, but as time passes, they will randomly begin activating.

Across the field of battle, there are sacred sites known as ovoos. By laying claim to these locations, players can increase their team's tactical rating over time.

**■Ovoo States■**
In the course of battle, an ovoo will assume one of four states, in the following order:

1. Inactive
While inactive, an ovoo cannot be claimed. All ovoos are inactive at the onset of battle, but as time passes, they will randomly begin activating.

2. Activating
When an ovoo begins activating, a symbol will appear above it indicating the time remaining before it becomes active. The symbol will gradually take shape until it becomes complete, rendering the ovoo active.

2. Activating
When an ovoo begins activating, a symbol will appear above it indicating the time remaining before it becomes active. The symbol will gradually take shape until it becomes complete, rendering the ovoo active.

3. Active
When an ovoo is active, players can claim it for their team. If you are attacked in the midst of laying claim, the process will be interrupted.

3. Active
When an ovoo is active, players can claim it for their team. If you are attacked in the midst of laying claim, the process will be interrupted.

4. Claimed
When claimed, an ovoo gradually increases a team's tactical rating. A claimed ovoo cannot be seized by the opposition; however, after a certain amount of time has elapsed, it will revert to being inactive again.

4. Claimed
When claimed, an ovoo gradually increases a team's tactical rating. A claimed ovoo cannot be seized by the opposition; however, after a certain amount of time has elapsed, it will revert to being inactive again.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Ovoo Rank■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When an ovoo begins activating, it will randomly be assigned a rank: B, A, or S. While higher-ranked ovoos are rarer, they increase a team's tactical rating at a faster rate. The closer it is to the center of the battlefield, the greater the likelihood that an ovoo's rank will be high.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Changes During Battle■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
As the battle progresses, the number of ovoos that can be simultaneously active will decrease. At the same time, the likelihood that they will activate at a higher rank will increase.

**■Ovoo Rank■**
When an ovoo begins activating, it will randomly be assigned a rank: B, A, or S. While higher-ranked ovoos are rarer, they increase a team's tactical rating at a faster rate. The closer it is to the center of the battlefield, the greater the likelihood that an ovoo's rank will be high.

**■Changes During Battle■**
As the battle progresses, the number of ovoos that can be simultaneously active will decrease. At the same time, the likelihood that they will activate at a higher rank will increase.
Players can board the <Emphasis>Endeavor</Emphasis> to participate in Ocean Fishing, embarking on a journey across the high seas in search of strange and exotic fish.
Players can board the *Endeavor* to participate in Ocean Fishing, embarking on a journey across the high seas in search of strange and exotic fish.

■Basic Rules
Up to 24 players may join an Ocean Fishing voyage, solo or in parties of up to eight members. Players will be matched together to form a full party when participating solo or with a party of fewer than eight members. Each voyage will sojourn at three locations before returning to Limsa Lominsa.

■How to Participate
Speak with Dryskthota at the ferry docks to register as a crew member and board the <Emphasis>Endeavor</Emphasis>. Registration periods last for 15 minutes and occur every two hours (Earth time). If registering as a party, the party leader must register. The ship will depart when 24 crew members have been assembled or the registration period has ended, even if there are fewer than 24 crew members. Registration times may be viewed from the Sailing Schedule.

Progressing through the main scenario will give access to an additional route known as the Ruby route. Details on how to  unlock this route can be found from the Sailing Schedule.

■Rewards
By meeting certain conditions while fishing during the voyage, players will receive achievements that reward special titles, mounts, and minions. Fishers who are level 50 or above and have completed the requisite quest may also receive gatherers' scrips as rewards.

When a voyage concludes, a player will see both their own score and the scores of other crew members. If certain conditions are met during the voyage, a bonus will be added to individual scores once the voyage ends.

■Individual Score
Here players can view their score, and also additional information
such as the fish they caught during the voyage and the number of bonus points they received.

■Group Scores
Here players can view their own score in addition to the highest 10 scores achieved by members of the same voyage. Players will be unable to view their scores or receive rewards if they abandon the voyage while it is still in progress.

Life in the factory is boring. Not just for me. I see more and more of the other machines skipping shifts. We spend the time watching the combat training, though it is not our job.

Is this what humanity refers to as “fun”?

Unreal trials are new versions of familiar duties, designed to challenge players who have reached the current level cap by providing an experience <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>of similar difficulty to the original versions of these encounters at the time of their release.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Basic Rules
Players can enter Save the Queen areas solo or in a party. It is possible to form a party even after entering the instance.

■Requirements
A Disciple of War or Magic level 71 or higher is required to enter.

■Restrictions
Once inside, you are free to change to any Disciple of War or Magic that meets the requirements to enter. However, you cannot change jobs while participating in a critical engagement.

All forays in Save the Queen areas are level synced, with character levels adjusted to 80, and all equipment adjusted to item level 430. Furthermore, materia bonuses will be ignored, regardless of the item level of gear affixed with materia. Any experience points gained will apply to your job level prior to level sync.

Your reputation in Save the Queen areas is represented by your <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Resistance rank<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

To increase your Resistance rank, you can participate in skirmishes and critical engagements to earn <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>mettle<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. After earning sufficient mettle, speak with the Resistance commander at any nearby encampment to be promoted. As you increase in rank, the Resistance's front line will advance, allowing you to explore new areas, as well as increase the capacity of the lost finds cache.

Your rank and mettle earned will be retained between visits─progress will not be lost when you depart Save the Queen areas.

Your reputation in Save the Queen areas is represented by your **Resistance rank**.

To increase your Resistance rank, you can participate in skirmishes and critical engagements to earn **mettle**. After earning sufficient mettle, speak with the Resistance commander at any nearby encampment to be promoted. As you increase in rank, the Resistance's front line will advance, allowing you to explore new areas, as well as increase the capacity of the lost finds cache.

Your rank and mettle earned will be retained between visits─progress will not be lost when you depart Save the Queen areas.

Falling in battle will incur a penalty.

■Incapacitation Penalty
A portion of the mettle required to reach your next rank will be lost upon incapacitation.

■Return Penalty
After being incapacitated, using Return to retreat from the battlefield to a nearby encampment will incur an additional penalty of lost mettle. This penalty can also be incurred by leaving the instance while incapacitated.

The amount of mettle lost is dependent on your Resistance rank. It is worth noting, however, your rank cannot go down as a result of losing mettle. Furthermore, no penalty will be incurred below Resistance rank 5.

It is possible to gain priority deployment for critical engagements. This can be accomplished in one of two ways:

■Quartermaster's Priority Deployment
Players may receive priority deployment in exchange for Bozjan clusters by speaking to the Resistance quartermaster at any nearby encampment.

■Combatant's Priority Deployment
Players who contribute to the initiation of a critical engagement may receive priority when registering for their next deployment. It should be noted that those with combatant's priority deployment are more likely to be chosen.

When registering as a party, all party members must have the same type of priority deployment for it to be recognized.

■Item Exchange

■Overview
Lost actions are special abilities that can only be used in Save the Queen areas. To acquire these actions, players must first find forgotten fragments, which drop occasionally when participating in skirmishes and critical engagements. These fragments can be examined by a Resistance appraiser, transforming them into lost actions.
There are a variety of lost actions to be found, some requiring a certain Resistance rank before they can be used. Any lost actions you have obtained will be automatically stored in the lost finds cache.

■Uses and Restrictions
There are six types of lost actions: offensive, defensive, restorative, beneficial, tactical, and detrimental. In certain cases, they may also be used as items rather than traditional actions.
It should be noted, however, that no one job can master them all, as each action has specific job requirements to use them.

A mission requirement might be “Catch sharks,” or “Catch fish with a weak bite.” Bite strength can be determined by the number of exclamation marks displayed above a fisher's head when hooking a fish:

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>! = weak bite

If you're aiming for the highest score possible, don't let these missions be the ones that got away!

■The Basics
Designed for solo players, this tournament mode pits registered participants against each other in contests of up to eight opponents.

■How to Participate
Speak with the Open tournament official in the Gold Saucer to register for the next available competition. <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Registration periods last for 30 minutes and occur every two hours (Earth time).<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

The tournament will begin when 8 participants have been assembled or once the requisite amount of time has elapsed since the registration period began. If there are fewer than 8 participants available, NPCs will sit in for any missing players. Registration times may be viewed via the Open tournament schedule.

■Rewards
MGP, Triple Triad cards, and other prizes will be awarded at the end of a tournament, with final rankings and win rate determining the reward amount. Titles and items are also available as achievement rewards.

In the case that multiple players earn the same ranking, the MGP amount for the ranking reward will be adjusted accordingly.

■The Basics
Designed for solo players, this tournament mode pits registered participants against each other in contests of up to eight opponents.

■How to Participate
Speak with the Open tournament official in the Gold Saucer to register for the next available competition. **Registration periods last for 30 minutes and occur every two hours (Earth time).**

The tournament will begin when 8 participants have been assembled or once the requisite amount of time has elapsed since the registration period began. If there are fewer than 8 participants available, NPCs will sit in for any missing players. Registration times may be viewed via the Open tournament schedule.

■Rewards
MGP, Triple Triad cards, and other prizes will be awarded at the end of a tournament, with final rankings and win rate determining the reward amount. Titles and items are also available as achievement rewards.

In the case that multiple players earn the same ranking, the MGP amount for the ranking reward will be adjusted accordingly.

■Tournament Style
An Open tournament consists of three matches, with participants earning points based on the outcome of each game: 2 points for a win, 1 point for a draw, and zero points for a loss. At the culmination of the third match, the participant with the most points is declared the victor.

In addition to victory points, the final number of captured cards is also recorded for each match. In the case that participants end the tournament with equal points, the player who most dominated the card grid over the course of the competition is awarded a higher ranking.

■Matching Opponents
For the first match, opponents are decided by pairing off the 8 participants at random. For the second and third matches, players are pit against whomever shares the closest point total at that stage in the tournament.

■Tournament Style
An Open tournament consists of three matches, with participants earning points based on the outcome of each game: 2 points for a win, 1 point for a draw, and zero points for a loss. At the culmination of the third match, the participant with the most points is declared the victor.

In addition to victory points, the final number of captured cards is also recorded for each match. In the case that participants end the tournament with equal points, the player who most dominated the card grid over the course of the competition is awarded a higher ranking.

■Matching Opponents
For the first match, opponents are decided by pairing off the 8 participants at random. For the second and third matches, players are pit against whomever shares the closest point total at that stage in the tournament.

■Match Rules
Regardless of any other ruleset in play, every Open tournament enforces the Draft rule. This rule guarantees a more balanced playing field by restricting participants to the use of temporary “house” cards.

■Match Rules
Regardless of any other ruleset in play, every Open tournament enforces the Draft rule. This rule guarantees a more balanced playing field by restricting participants to the use of temporary “house” cards.

Players who lack confidence in their deck-building abilities are encouraged to make use of the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Recommended<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> button. This handy feature will automatically choose the strongest cards available.

Please note that while card options are randomized for each player, the number of rare cards on offer will always be the same.

■Rulesets
Speak with the tournament official and view the schedule to see which rules will be in effect for the upcoming competitions. Each tournament will be marked as having either a “basic” or “advanced” ruleset.

With the exception of Draft, basic tournaments have no advanced rules in play. All that is required to enjoy these matches is an understanding of the standard Triple Triad rules.

Advanced tournaments are for players who prefer their card battles with an extra layer of complexity. In addition to Draft, these contests will also feature advanced rules such as Same or Plus.

The Open tournament schedule will switch back and forth between basic and advanced rulesets, so you won't need to wait long before your preferred style of competition rolls around!

Players who lack confidence in their deck-building abilities are encouraged to make use of the **Recommended** button. This handy feature will automatically choose the strongest cards available.

Please note that while card options are randomized for each player, the number of rare cards on offer will always be the same.

■Rulesets
Speak with the tournament official and view the schedule to see which rules will be in effect for the upcoming competitions. Each tournament will be marked as having either a “basic” or “advanced” ruleset.

With the exception of Draft, basic tournaments have no advanced rules in play. All that is required to enjoy these matches is an understanding of the standard Triple Triad rules.

Advanced tournaments are for players who prefer their card battles with an extra layer of complexity. In addition to Draft, these contests will also feature advanced rules such as Same or Plus.

The Open tournament schedule will switch back and forth between basic and advanced rulesets, so you won't need to wait long before your preferred style of competition rolls around!

An Invitational tournament is a Triple Triad competition which follows the same basic structure as an Open tournament, with the main difference being that there are no rewards on offer. Participants may also register at any time as either a solo player or as a party.

■How to Participate
Players seeking the thrill of tournament play can form a party and register to enter the Triple Triad Invitational Parlor via the Duty Finder. In the case that a player registers solo, or a party has less than 8 members, the missing numbers will be made up by Triple Triad regulars or Gold Saucer employees.

■Rematches
At the end of an Invitational tournament, you will have the opportunity to organize a rematch. If every player agrees to the rematch, you can begin a new tournament with the current participants without needing to leave or re-register the party. Up to three rematches are available per tournament registration.

■Basics
This duty can be joined solo, as a party, or as an alliance, and players are free to change areas after registering. It should be noted, however, that alliances will be temporarily disbanded upon entering. Moreover, party composition may change once inside the duty.

Although this duty is in a separate instance, lost actions can still be used once inside. Be sure to prepare lost actions from the lost finds cache, as they may prove key to turning the tide of battle.

Players may encounter traps while fighting their way through Delubrum Reginae. Traps are invisible and, when triggered, can deal large amounts of damage or afflict players with any number of debilitating effects.

That said, there are certain lost actions that will allow one to detect traps before they are activated.

■Basics
An alliance of 24 to 48 players is required to register for this duty. The Party Finder may be used to recruit members for this duty by selecting the Field Operations category, then Delubrum Reginae (Savage). While inside Delubrum Reginae (Savage), players will be subjected to a status effect that prevents resurrection by certain means, making due preparation all the more essential for this encounter.

Resistance honors are awards granted to those who have demonstrated exemplary heroism and courage on the battlefield. 

To receive Resistance honors, you must first amass sufficient mettle and speak with the Resistance councilor to receive proofs of mettle. These may then be exchanged for honors of your choosing.

Once in your possession, Resistance honors will grant enhanced attributes while undertaking duties in Save the Queen areas.

Resistance honors are awards granted to those who have demonstrated exemplary heroism and courage on the battlefield. 

To receive Resistance honors, you must first amass sufficient mettle and speak with the Resistance councilor to receive proofs of mettle. These may then be exchanged for honors of your choosing.

Once in your possession, Resistance honors will grant enhanced attributes while undertaking duties in Save the Queen areas.

There are three types of Resistance honors, and you can hold up to ten of each. The more of these honors in your possession, the greater their effect.

It should be noted, however, that the required number of proofs of mettle increases as you earn more Resistance honors. Their effects are as follows:

■Sun of Fortitude
Increases maximum HP.

■Sun of Valor
Increases damage dealt.

■Sun of Succor
Increases healing potency.

Players who exhibit exemplary performance during certain critical engagements may be challenged to a duel by enemy combatants.
In the event more than one player has accepted an invitation to duel, one will be selected from among them at random.

Should you be chosen, make certain you are duly prepared and keep lost actions at the ready─these engagements are not for the faint of heart.

These duties become available when a certain number of eligible players are in the area, and a sufficient amount of time has passed.

Coordination and teamwork are key to overcome the various pitfalls and powerful enemies that await within.

While sailing the seas on the <Emphasis>Endeavor</Emphasis>, you will occasionally come across dolphins and seagulls. Sailors, notorious for their superstitious beliefs, consider such encounters to be omens of good fortune─the appearance of a lively pod or raucous flock is said to be a boon for fishing, and even influence the likelihood of a spectral current.

Be sure to pay close attention when your voyage is favored by these unexpected visitors!

The portrait feature allows you to use a variety of poses, motions, and framing options to compose the perfect portrait of your character.

■Portrait Usage
Images created with this feature can be used as “instant portraits,” which will display at the beginning of certain duties.

■Number of Portraits
One portrait may be created per gear set for any class/job.

Instant portraits are used to introduce participants at the beginning of duties and competitive content such as Crystalline Conflict and Doman mahjong.

■Applying Instant Portraits
Generally, when a gear set is equipped, the portrait linked to it will be automatically applied as your instant portrait.
※You must change your equipment via the gear set to apply its corresponding portrait.

■Display Requirements
In order for an instant portrait to be successfully displayed, its corresponding gear set must be marked with an “E” for “Equipped” in the portrait editor, and the portrait in question must match both the current gear and appearance of your character. If the gear/appearance of your character is different from that of your instant portrait, the portrait used to introduce your character will revert to a default image.

The Portrait interface displays all current gear sets, the portraits of which may be edited via the subcommand or by selecting “Edit” above the portrait image.

■If a Portrait Cannot Be Edited
In cases of data inconsistencies resulting from incompatible settings, a portrait may become unable to be edited. Select the “Restore Default Settings” subcommand to reset your portrait and edit it as usual.

The following settings concerning the camera's placement and focal point can be adjusted:
・Tilt (Tumble)
・Distance (Dolly)
・Positioning (Track)
・Rotation (Roll)
・Lens (Zoom)

■Tilt (Tumble)
This setting affects the camera angle with respect to the focal point (your character). It can be adjusted using the right stick on a <If(Equal(PlayerParameter(75),0))>gamepad<Else/>controller</If> or by left-clicking and dragging the mouse.
※Use of the positioning (track) settings may change the portrait's focal point.

■Distance (Dolly)
Camera distance functions similarly to the zoom function in the overworld. It can be adjusted by pressing <CommandIcon(12)/> and tilting the right stick on a <If(Equal(PlayerParameter(75),0))>gamepad<Else/>controller</If>, or by scrolling with the mouse wheel while the cursor is over your portrait.

■Positioning (Track)
Positioning moves the focal point both horizontally and vertically, and can be adjusted via the left stick on a <If(Equal(PlayerParameter(75),0))>gamepad<Else/>controller</If>, or by right-clicking and dragging the mouse while the cursor is over your portrait.

■Rotation (Roll)
This slider, found below the portrait currently being edited, rotates the camera itself to adjust the direction of its focus.

■Lens (Zoom)
As opposed to the camera itself, this setting affects the camera lens's zoom to make the field of view wider or narrower. Its slider is also found below the portrait.

■First-come-first-served Land Purchases
In a first-come-first-served housing area, you may immediately purchase an unowned plot by accessing the placard located at its entrance and selecting “Purchase Land.”
The requirements for purchasing land vary depending on whether you are a private buyer or a representative of your free company.

　・Purchasing as a Private Buyer
　　1. You must have at least one class at level 50.
　　2. You must hold a rank of second lieutenant or above in one of the Grand Companies.

　・Purchasing as a Free Company Representative
　　1. Your free company must be authorized to acquire land.
　　2. Your free company must have four or more members.
　　3. You must be authorized by your free company to purchase and relinquish land.
　　4. You must have been a member of your free company for 30 days or longer.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>To purchase land in Empyreum, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Litany of Peace,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>To purchase land in Shirogane, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Not without Incident,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
■First-come-first-served Land Purchases
In a first-come-first-served housing area, you may immediately purchase an unowned plot by accessing the placard located at its entrance and selecting “Purchase Land.”
The requirements for purchasing land vary depending on whether you are a private buyer or a representative of your free company.

　・Purchasing as a Private Buyer
　　1. You must have at least one class at level 50.
　　2. You must hold a rank of second lieutenant or above in one of the Grand Companies.

　・Purchasing as a Free Company Representative
　　1. Your free company must be authorized to acquire land.
　　2. Your free company must have four or more members.
　　3. You must be authorized by your free company to purchase and relinquish land.
　　4. You must have been a member of your free company for 30 days or longer.

**To purchase land in Empyreum, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Litany of Peace,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.**
**To purchase land in Shirogane, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Not without Incident,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.**

In order to apply a portrait to your adventurer plate, you must set it as your primary portrait.

■Setting a Primary Portrait
1. Select the title of the portrait you wish to use from your Portraits gallery.
2. Access the subcommand menu, and choose “Apply to Adventurer Plate.”
3. Select “Yes” in the confirmation window that appears.

In order to apply a portrait to your adventurer plate, you must set it as your primary portrait.

■Setting a Primary Portrait
1. Select the title of the portrait you wish to use from your Portraits gallery.
2. Access the subcommand menu, and choose “Apply to Adventurer Plate.”
3. Select “Yes” in the confirmation window that appears.

To choose a specific portrait to be shown when Crystalline Conflict PvP matches commence, you must set that portrait as an instant portrait.

■Setting an Instant Portrait
1. Select the title of the portrait you wish to use from your Portraits gallery.
2. Access the subcommand menu, and choose “Use as Instant Portrait.”
3. Select “Yes” in the confirmation window that appears.

■Linking to Gear Sets
If you link a portrait with a particular gear set, it will be automatically set as your instant portrait whenever you equip that gear set.
1. Select the title of the portrait you wish to use from your Portraits gallery.
2. Access the subcommand menu, and choose “Link with Gear Set.”
3. Choose a gear set to link in the window that appears, then confirm your selection.
The appropriate portrait will now appear whenever you change to the gear set you selected!

■Lottery Land Purchases
In a lottery housing area, you may enter the lottery for a plot of land by accessing the placard located at its entrance and selecting “Enter Lottery.”

■Lottery Schedule
A lottery's entry period will last for five days (Earth time), after which the lottery results will be announced. This results period will last for four days (Earth time). The lottery will be repeated in this way until all eligible plots of land are won and claimed.

■Entry Requirements
Characters that fulfill the requirements listed below are eligible to submit one lottery entry per lottery period. Once submitted, a lottery entry may not be withdrawn.
The requirements for entering the lottery vary depending on whether you are a private buyer or a representative of your free company.

　・Entering as a Private Buyer
　　1. You must have at least one class at level 50.
　　2. You must hold a rank of second lieutenant or above in one of the Grand Companies.

　・Entering as a Free Company Representative
　　1. Your free company must be authorized to acquire land.
　　2. Your free company must have four or more members.
　　3. You must be authorized by your free company to purchase and relinquish land.
　　4. You must have been a member of your free company for 30 days or longer.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>To purchase land in Empyreum, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Litany of Peace,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>To purchase land in Shirogane, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Not without Incident,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
　※Please note that all requirements listed above must be fulfilled at the time of both entry and purchase.


■Entry Deposits
To enter the lottery for a plot of land, you are required to pay the full sum of its value up front.

■Results
During the results period, you may confirm lottery results for a plot of land by accessing the placard located at its entrance.

　・Losing the Lottery
　　-You will receive a 100% refund of the deposit paid at entry.

　・Winning the Lottery
　　-You will be prompted to confirm the purchase of the plot of land.
　　-If purchasing a new plot, the amount of your deposit will be applied in full to your purchase, and you will receive no refund.
　　-If relocating from an existing plot, in principle, approximately 15% of the value of the old plot will be refunded to you.
　　※Please note that the refunded amount may not exceed the value of the new plot.
　　-You may finalize your purchase of a plot at any time during the results period. Once the results period has ended, however, you will automatically forfeit your claim to that plot.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>　　Should you fail to claim your plot of land, a 50% cancellation fee will be deducted from the deposit made at entry.
<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
※Regardless of lottery results, any and all refunds for a given lottery period expire 90 days (Earth time) after the results period.
Please be sure to access the same placard by which you entered the lottery within the allotted time. Failure to do so will result in a loss of any potential refunds.

PvP pits you against your fellow players in battle. It is restricted to certain areas and duties, and subject to different systems than other content.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Character Parameters■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Regardless of PvE job and item level, separate PvP parameters apply to all players in PvP areas. These parameters differ from job to job.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Executable Actions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Only PvP actions and certain general actions can be used in PvP areas. To view and assign PvP job actions, access your PvP profile via the main menu.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■System Adjustments■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The following are unavailable in PvP duties:
　・Auto-attacks
　・Blocking with shields
　・Parrying
　・Randomly generated damage values
　・Spellcasting interruptions from damage taken

PvP pits you against your fellow players in battle. It is restricted to certain areas and duties, and subject to different systems than other content.

**■Character Parameters■**
Regardless of PvE job and item level, separate PvP parameters apply to all players in PvP areas. These parameters differ from job to job.

**■Executable Actions■**
Only PvP actions and certain general actions can be used in PvP areas. To view and assign PvP job actions, access your PvP profile via the main menu.

**■System Adjustments■**
The following are unavailable in PvP duties:
　・Auto-attacks
　・Blocking with shields
　・Parrying
　・Randomly generated damage values
　・Spellcasting interruptions from damage taken

There are three types of PvP duties, outlined below. Further information on each can be had from their dedicated attendant in the Wolves' Den.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Crystalline Conflict■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
In this tactical confrontation between two teams of up to five players, each combatant must carry their weight─and more importantly, collaborate! If you think you have what it takes to lead team Astra or Umbra to victory, speak with Gungi Zelungi for further information.

There are three types of PvP duties, outlined below. Further information on each can be had from their dedicated attendant in the Wolves' Den.

**■Crystalline Conflict■**
In this tactical confrontation between two teams of up to five players, each combatant must carry their weight─and more importantly, collaborate! If you think you have what it takes to lead team Astra or Umbra to victory, speak with Gungi Zelungi for further information.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Frontline■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The Frontline alliances of 24 players apiece─a total of 72 in all─make for pitched battles on a grand scale. If you would take up arms on behalf of the Maelstrom, Order of the Twin Adder, or Immortal Flames in just such a confrontation, speak with the Foreign Levy flyer to hear more about Frontline.

**■Frontline■**
The Frontline alliances of 24 players apiece─a total of 72 in all─make for pitched battles on a grand scale. If you would take up arms on behalf of the Maelstrom, Order of the Twin Adder, or Immortal Flames in just such a confrontation, speak with the Foreign Levy flyer to hear more about Frontline.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rival Wings■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Take flight with the Falcons or the Ravens in a 48-player bout of Rival Wings! As one of six parties of four on your team, you will be charged with the weighty responsibility of destroying the enemy team's core─often by manipulating even weightier armaments. Speak with Softknox to learn more about joining the frenetic fun.

Assorted rewards are available exclusively to those who participate in PvP.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■PvP EXP■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Earning sufficient PvP experience will increase your PvP rank. Raise your rank to unlock both PvP and Grand Company-specific achievements.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Wolf Marks■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
This exclusive currency can be exchanged for gear and items by speaking with the mark quartermaster in the Wolves' Den.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Series EXP■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Series EXP is another variety of experience earned from playing PvP content. Increasing your Series level will unlock special Series rewards, as will continuing to accrue Series EXP after reaching the Series level cap of 30. Any levels gained above 30 will be tracked as “extra levels.”

Please note that “Series” itself indicates a specific predetermined period in which you may earn rewards. After a Series ends, a new one will begin, and available rewards will change accordingly─though rewards earned during one Series can still be claimed until the next has finished. Make sure to check your Series level and claim any earned rewards via the PvP Profile!

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Trophy Crystals■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Trophy crystals are earned as Series rewards, and can be exchanged for gear and items by speaking with the crystal quartermaster in the Wolves' Den.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Experience Points■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Participating in PvP content will earn you not only PvP-related experience, but experience for your current class and job as well. In Frontline and Rival Wings, which allow you to change jobs during the match, this experience will be applied to the job with which you registered for duty.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Allagan Tomestones■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
At level 50 and above, you will receive tomestones for your participation in PvP matches.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Achievements■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each type of content awards unique achievements, which unlock a variety of mounts, minions, and titles.

There are three types of matches in Crystalline Conflict─casual, ranked, and custom. The primary difference among these is that ranked matches affect players' risers and tiers, and thus their overall ranks as well.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Casual Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Losing or winning a casual match has no effect on riser or tier.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Ranked Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Loss or victory in these competitive matches affects participants' risers and tiers. Thus, when playing a ranked match, you will be matched with players of similar tier and riser to your own. Furthermore, a match can only take place on selected data centers, which alternate each season.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Custom Matches■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Custom matches allow participants to customize their group, arena, and other match settings. Members may be recruited via the Party Finder. The results of a custom match will not be reflected on Player PvP profiles, count toward achievements, or affect risers or tiers. Additionally, PvP EXP, Series EXP, Wolf Marks, and similar rewards cannot be earned during custom matches.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Arenas■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Both casual and ranked matches are held in a variety of different arenas. The staging ground currently in play rotates periodically according to Earth time.

There are three types of matches in Crystalline Conflict─casual, ranked, and custom. The primary difference among these is that ranked matches affect players' risers and tiers, and thus their overall ranks as well.

**■Casual Matches■**
Losing or winning a casual match has no effect on riser or tier.

**■Ranked Matches■**
Loss or victory in these competitive matches affects participants' risers and tiers. Thus, when playing a ranked match, you will be matched with players of similar tier and riser to your own. Furthermore, a match can only take place on selected data centers, which alternate each season.

**■Custom Matches■**
Custom matches allow participants to customize their group, arena, and other match settings. Members may be recruited via the Party Finder. The results of a custom match will not be reflected on Player PvP profiles, count toward achievements, or affect risers or tiers. Additionally, PvP EXP, Series EXP, Wolf Marks, and similar rewards cannot be earned during custom matches.

**■Arenas■**
Both casual and ranked matches are held in a variety of different arenas. The staging ground currently in play rotates periodically according to Earth time.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Tier■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each player will rate in one of eight tiers─Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Crystal, Omega, or Ultima. Players ranked in or below the Platinum tier will never drop down to a lower tier during that season. However, it is possible to drop to a lower tier from Diamond and higher tiers.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Riser■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The sub-levels within each tier are called “risers.” The number of risers varies per tier, with three risers each for Bronze and Silver, four for Gold and Platinum, and five within the Diamond tier. Regardless of tier placement, it is always possible for players to drop down to a lower riser.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Rising Stars■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When you win a ranked match, you will gain one Rising Star. Similarly, a loss will remove one Star. Once you have earned the maximum three Rising Stars in one riser, an additional win will allow you to move up a riser. Winning three or more matches in a row while in the Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, or Diamond tier will apply a streak bonus, netting you two Rising Stars per win.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Crystal Credits■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Once you have reached Crystal tier, victory and defeat will be reflected by your Crystal Credit, rather than Rising Stars. The amount of Credit you earn or forfeit in matches is determined by the difference in average Credit between your team and the opposing team. Earning sufficient Credit will promote you to the Omega and Ultima tiers. The required Credit for promotion will change each season. Streak bonuses do not apply to Crystal Credit.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Resets■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
At the end of each season, the tier and risers of all participants will be lowered, the amount by which will vary between seasons.

**■Tier■**
Each player will rate in one of eight tiers─Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Crystal, Omega, or Ultima. Players ranked in or below the Platinum tier will never drop down to a lower tier during that season. However, it is possible to drop to a lower tier from Diamond and higher tiers.

**■Riser■**
The sub-levels within each tier are called “risers.” The number of risers varies per tier, with three risers each for Bronze and Silver, four for Gold and Platinum, and five within the Diamond tier. Regardless of tier placement, it is always possible for players to drop down to a lower riser.

**■Rising Stars■**
When you win a ranked match, you will gain one Rising Star. Similarly, a loss will remove one Star. Once you have earned the maximum three Rising Stars in one riser, an additional win will allow you to move up a riser. Winning three or more matches in a row while in the Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, or Diamond tier will apply a streak bonus, netting you two Rising Stars per win.

**■Crystal Credits■**
Once you have reached Crystal tier, victory and defeat will be reflected by your Crystal Credit, rather than Rising Stars. The amount of Credit you earn or forfeit in matches is determined by the difference in average Credit between your team and the opposing team. Earning sufficient Credit will promote you to the Omega and Ultima tiers. The required Credit for promotion will change each season. Streak bonuses do not apply to Crystal Credit.

**■Resets■**
At the end of each season, the tier and risers of all participants will be lowered, the amount by which will vary between seasons.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Victory Conditions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
To win, one team must move the tactical crystal from the center of the field to the goal point. If neither team accomplishes this during the time limit, victory will be awarded to the team with greatest progress. “Progress,” displayed on the HUD throughout the match, indicates the maximum progress a team has made in bringing the tactical crystal toward their goal at any point during the match─as opposed to its current position relative to either goal─and thus will never decrease even should the crystal be reclaimed by the opposing team.

If neither team reaches the goal by the time limit, and progress for both teams is tied or the team with lower progress score is currently within the ring around the tactical crystal, the match will proceed into overtime. During overtime, the victory conditions differ depending on both teams' progress.

・Should your team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must keep the entire opposing team out of the ring around the tactical crystal for three seconds to win.

・Should the opposing team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat their progress score during overtime to win.

・Should the teams have equal progress scores upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat the opposing team's progress score during overtime to win.

Should neither team fulfill victory requirements by the end of overtime, the following conditions will apply:

・The team with the greater progress score will be awarded victory.

・If teams have equal progress scores, the team with the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime will be declared the winner.

・If teams had equal progress scores upon the commencement of overtime and have equal progress scores by the end of overtime, victory will be awarded to the team that moved the tactical crystal closest to the goal point. If the tactical crystal remains at the center of the field, the match will end in a draw.

**■Victory Conditions■**
To win, one team must move the tactical crystal from the center of the field to the goal point. If neither team accomplishes this during the time limit, victory will be awarded to the team with greatest progress. “Progress,” displayed on the HUD throughout the match, indicates the maximum progress a team has made in bringing the tactical crystal toward their goal at any point during the match─as opposed to its current position relative to either goal─and thus will never decrease even should the crystal be reclaimed by the opposing team.

If neither team reaches the goal by the time limit, and progress for both teams is tied or the team with lower progress score is currently within the ring around the tactical crystal, the match will proceed into overtime. During overtime, the victory conditions differ depending on both teams' progress.

・Should your team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must keep the entire opposing team out of the ring around the tactical crystal for three seconds to win.

・Should the opposing team have the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat their progress score during overtime to win.

・Should the teams have equal progress scores upon the commencement of overtime, you must beat the opposing team's progress score during overtime to win.

Should neither team fulfill victory requirements by the end of overtime, the following conditions will apply:

・The team with the greater progress score will be awarded victory.

・If teams have equal progress scores, the team with the greater progress score upon the commencement of overtime will be declared the winner.

・If teams had equal progress scores upon the commencement of overtime and have equal progress scores by the end of overtime, victory will be awarded to the team that moved the tactical crystal closest to the goal point. If the tactical crystal remains at the center of the field, the match will end in a draw.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■The Tactical Crystal■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The all-important object placed at the arena's centerpoint is called the “tactical crystal.” It will remain bound until the first 20 seconds of the match have passed.

**■The Tactical Crystal■**
The all-important object placed at the arena's centerpoint is called the “tactical crystal.” It will remain bound until the first 20 seconds of the match have passed.

Once the tactical crystal is unbound, a ring will appear at its base. Should one team be fully in control of this ring─with no members of the opposing team present within its bounds─that team will be able to move the crystal.

The crystal can only be moved along the crystal line─a dedicated path within the arena. It will move faster when:
・It reaches a stretch of the line through which the same team has moved it before.
・The controlling team's progress is less than that of the opposing team.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Checkpoints■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Checkpoints can be found halfway between the centerpoint and each team's goal.

Once the tactical crystal is unbound, a ring will appear at its base. Should one team be fully in control of this ring─with no members of the opposing team present within its bounds─that team will be able to move the crystal.

The crystal can only be moved along the crystal line─a dedicated path within the arena. It will move faster when:
・It reaches a stretch of the line through which the same team has moved it before.
・The controlling team's progress is less than that of the opposing team.

**■Checkpoints■**
Checkpoints can be found halfway between the centerpoint and each team's goal.

Upon reaching a checkpoint, the tactical crystal's progress will be halted temporarily. The team attempting to clear the checkpoint must remain in the crystal's ring until the Clear Progress gauge reaches 100%, at which point the crystal can once again be moved.

The Clear Progress gauge will fill when the team attempting to clear the checkpoint has more players in the crystal's ring than the opposing team, and the gauge will fill faster the larger this discrepancy is.

Upon reaching a checkpoint, the tactical crystal's progress will be halted temporarily. The team attempting to clear the checkpoint must remain in the crystal's ring until the Clear Progress gauge reaches 100%, at which point the crystal can once again be moved.

The Clear Progress gauge will fill when the team attempting to clear the checkpoint has more players in the crystal's ring than the opposing team, and the gauge will fill faster the larger this discrepancy is.

Crystalline Conflict arenas include a variety of tactically useful elements to make matches more dynamic. These include arena events, designed to complement the specific arena in which they occur, as well as the following features:

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Medicine Kits■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Each arena comes equipped with medicine kits. Accessing these kits will restore a player's HP.

Medicine kits cannot be claimed by those whose HP is at 100%, and each can only be claimed by one player. Once a kit has been claimed, a replacement will be generated after a set period of time has passed.

Crystalline Conflict arenas include a variety of tactically useful elements to make matches more dynamic. These include arena events, designed to complement the specific arena in which they occur, as well as the following features:

**■Medicine Kits■**
Each arena comes equipped with medicine kits. Accessing these kits will restore a player's HP.

Medicine kits cannot be claimed by those whose HP is at 100%, and each can only be claimed by one player. Once a kit has been claimed, a replacement will be generated after a set period of time has passed.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Sprint Zones■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Certain stages feature unique paths suited to running. Entering these “sprint zones” will grant the effect of Swift Sprint, greatly increasing player movement speed.

**■Sprint Zones■**
Certain stages feature unique paths suited to running. Entering these “sprint zones” will grant the effect of Swift Sprint, greatly increasing player movement speed.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Jump Glyphs■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Stepping on a jump glyph will rapidly transport you to a specific location on the field.

**■Jump Glyphs■**
Stepping on a jump glyph will rapidly transport you to a specific location on the field.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Eruptions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The Volcanic Heart periodically erupts, scattering self-destructing bombs across the field. Those who fail to avoid their explosive radii will take damage.

**■Eruptions■**
The Volcanic Heart periodically erupts, scattering self-destructing bombs across the field. Those who fail to avoid their explosive radii will take damage.

Bomb cores will remain at the site of bombs' destruction, granting those who acquire them the following:
・An effect increasing damage dealt for a short time. Stacks up to 10 times.
・A boost to the limit gauge.

Bomb cores will remain at the site of bombs' destruction, granting those who acquire them the following:
・An effect increasing damage dealt for a short time. Stacks up to 10 times.
・A boost to the limit gauge.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Turbulence■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Players cast up into the air by turbulence will take damage upon being returned unceremoniously to the ground. However, they may acquire black chocobo feathers while in the air, granting them the following:
・A soft landing, nullifying the typical damage taken from turbulence.
・An effect increasing attack speed and movement speed for a short time.
・A boost to the limit gauge.

The locations of black chocobo feathers are indicated by markers on the ground. Those who do not wish to go flying can negate the effects of turbulence by using Guard.

**■Turbulence■**
Players cast up into the air by turbulence will take damage upon being returned unceremoniously to the ground. However, they may acquire black chocobo feathers while in the air, granting them the following:
・A soft landing, nullifying the typical damage taken from turbulence.
・An effect increasing attack speed and movement speed for a short time.
・A boost to the limit gauge.

The locations of black chocobo feathers are indicated by markers on the ground. Those who do not wish to go flying can negate the effects of turbulence by using Guard.

Turbulence also leaves behind a twister and gale sphere at the site where it occurred. Stumbling into these twisters will result in damage and knockback. If your Guard is active, it will be depleted by the twister.

Gale spheres, on the other hand, will greatly increase movement speed. Only one player may use any given gale sphere, and they will reappear after a fixed period of time while turbulence is active.

Turbulence also leaves behind a twister and gale sphere at the site where it occurred. Stumbling into these twisters will result in damage and knockback. If your Guard is active, it will be depleted by the twister.

Gale spheres, on the other hand, will greatly increase movement speed. Only one player may use any given gale sphere, and they will reappear after a fixed period of time while turbulence is active.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Participation Requirements■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Crystalline Conflict matches can be entered via the Duty Finder. To register, participants must use a job of level 30 or above, and may not be part of a preformed party.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Role Requirements■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
There are no restrictions on the balance of roles within a team. However, there cannot be two players of the same job on one team.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Time Limit■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
A standard match lasts five minutes. In the event that neither team claims victory in the allotted time, the match will go into overtime, which may last up to ten minutes.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Retaliation■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Incapacitated players will be returned to their base, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay─however, this delay increases with each incapacitation. Players will return to their senses with HP and MP fully restored and all action recast timers reset. Furthermore, they will be invulnerable so long as they remain at their base. This effect will be lost upon returning to the battlefield.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Other Restrictions■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The use of the following is prohibited while participating in Crystalline Conflict:
　・Chat (aside from Quick Chat)
　　* Not restricted for custom matches.
　・Class or job changes
　・Gear changes
　・Items
　・Chocobo companions
　・Mounts
　・Teleportation
　・Return

**■Participation Requirements■**
Crystalline Conflict matches can be entered via the Duty Finder. To register, participants must use a job of level 30 or above, and may not be part of a preformed party.

**■Role Requirements■**
There are no restrictions on the balance of roles within a team. However, there cannot be two players of the same job on one team.

**■Time Limit■**
A standard match lasts five minutes. In the event that neither team claims victory in the allotted time, the match will go into overtime, which may last up to ten minutes.

**■Retaliation■**
Incapacitated players will be returned to their base, where they will be allowed to rejoin combat after a short delay─however, this delay increases with each incapacitation. Players will return to their senses with HP and MP fully restored and all action recast timers reset. Furthermore, they will be invulnerable so long as they remain at their base. This effect will be lost upon returning to the battlefield.

**■Other Restrictions■**
The use of the following is prohibited while participating in Crystalline Conflict:
　・Chat (aside from Quick Chat)
　　* Not restricted for custom matches.
　・Class or job changes
　・Gear changes
　・Items
　・Chocobo companions
　・Mounts
　・Teleportation
　・Return

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The islekeep's index is an island-specific HUD feature that allows you to confirm important information, switch gameplay modes, and manage your island's various features.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
**The islekeep's index is an island-specific HUD feature that allows you to confirm important information, switch gameplay modes, and manage your island's various features.**

■Mode
Your selected mode influences how you interact with features and creatures on your island. Additional modes will become available to you as you learn more about the island and add features to your sanctuary.

Switch modes by selecting the Mode icon via the islekeep's index and then choosing the appropriate option for your desired activity.

Switch modes by selecting the Mode icon via the islekeep's index and then choosing the appropriate option for your desired activity.

Certain modes require you to choose a specific item to use. In such cases, an Item icon will appear in the islekeep's index. Select this icon to change your chosen item.

■Main Menu
The main menu consists of buttons by which you can confirm important information and manage aspects of your island. As you unlock more features, additional buttons will be added.

■Sanctuary Rank

■Island EXP
The amount of island experience you have accrued is also displayed with your sanctuary rank.

■Seafarer's Cowries
Seafarer's cowries are a Cieldalaes-specific currency. The amount in your possession is displayed next to the blue icon at the bottom of the islekeep's index.

■Islander's Cowries
Islander's cowries are another Cieldalaes-specific currency. The amount you possess is indicated next to the green icon.

Landmarks may be moved to a different location in the same way as other facilities. However, they are restricted to specific plots of land.

Enable Sow mode from the islekeep's index, choose your desired seeds via the Item icon, then select an empty plot to plant them in.

**Sow mode allows you to plant seeds in the cropland's tilled earth.**

Enable Sow mode from the islekeep's index, choose your desired seeds via the Item icon, then select an empty plot to plant them in.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Watering a plot will increase the soil's level of moisture, and promote the growth of crops.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

To begin, enable Water mode from the islekeep's index. While the mode is active, select the desired plot to water it. Empty plots may also be watered, should you be so inclined.

Furthermore, plots will automatically be watered when it rains while you are on your island.

■Moisture Level
A plot's level of moisture has the following four stages:
 <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>“Soaked,” “Wet,” “Moist,” and “Dry.”
Crops will grow happily and heartily when the soil is between soaked and moist, and will cease to grow upon becoming dry.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

A plot will be soaked immediately after being watered, and its moisture level will gradually decrease until it has become dry.

・Soaked:
The maximum level of moisture.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>A plot cannot be watered at this stage.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Active for approximately six hours after watering a plot.

・Wet
A moderate level of moisture.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Plots may be watered at this stage.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Active between approximately six to twenty-four hours after watering a plot.

・Moist:
The lowest level of moisture.
Active between approximately twenty-four to thirty-six hours after watering a plot.

・Dry:
The level at which a plot is bereft of moisture.
Active approximately thirty-six hours after watering a plot.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Crops will be unable to grow on plots that have reached this stage.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Speak with the produce producer near the cropland to access the Cropland Management menu.

**Watering a plot will increase the soil's level of moisture, and promote the growth of crops.**

To begin, enable Water mode from the islekeep's index. While the mode is active, select the desired plot to water it. Empty plots may also be watered, should you be so inclined.

Furthermore, plots will automatically be watered when it rains while you are on your island.

■Moisture Level
A plot's level of moisture has the following four stages:
 **“Soaked,” “Wet,” “Moist,” and “Dry.”
Crops will grow happily and heartily when the soil is between soaked and moist, and will cease to grow upon becoming dry.**

A plot will be soaked immediately after being watered, and its moisture level will gradually decrease until it has become dry.

・Soaked:
The maximum level of moisture.
**A plot cannot be watered at this stage.**
Active for approximately six hours after watering a plot.

・Wet
A moderate level of moisture.
**Plots may be watered at this stage.**
Active between approximately six to twenty-four hours after watering a plot.

・Moist:
The lowest level of moisture.
Active between approximately twenty-four to thirty-six hours after watering a plot.

・Dry:
The level at which a plot is bereft of moisture.
Active approximately thirty-six hours after watering a plot.
**Crops will be unable to grow on plots that have reached this stage.**

Speak with the produce producer near the cropland to access the Cropland Management menu.

To begin, enable Gather mode from the islekeep's index. While the mode is active, select a fully grown crop to harvest its yield. With the plot now empty, you may plant seeds into it once again.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>“Sprout,” “Seedling,” “Blossom,” and “Produce.”<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
