<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>You may entrust the care of your crops to the gardener, who will arrange for a team of green-thumbed mammets to sow, water, and gather yields in your stead.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Speak with the produce producer after obtaining the appropriate mammet-sized tools to get started.

Please note that you will not receive island experience points when retrieving yields harvested through the gardening service.

■Entrusting Your Crops
From the Cropland Management menu, choose the plot you wish the gardener to maintain, and select the “Entrust to Gardener” subcommand.

If seeds have already been planted in the plot you select, the same type of seed will automatically be applied for further cultivation. If the plot you select is currently empty, choose which seeds you wish to cultivate.

A daily fee of seafarer's cowries will be required to maintain your crops. Maintenance will be suspended if seafarer's cowries are insufficient, your supply of seeds has been exhausted, or if yields have accumulated to the maximum of 50. Plots will be marked “Under Care” if maintenance is proceeding smoothly and “Care Halted” if maintenance has been suspended.

The fee of seafarer's cowries will be automatically collected each day at <ResetTime>12</ResetTime><If(TopLevelParameter(218))><Switch(TopLevelParameter(218))><Case(1)>1</Case><Case(2)>2</Case><Case(3)>3</Case><Case(4)>4</Case><Case(5)>5</Case><Case(6)>6</Case><Case(7)>7</Case><Case(8)>8</Case><Case(9)>9</Case><Case(10)>10</Case><Case(11)>11</Case><Case(12)>12</Case><Case(13)>1</Case><Case(14)>2</Case><Case(15)>3</Case><Case(16)>4</Case><Case(17)>5</Case><Case(18)>6</Case><Case(19)>7</Case><Case(20)>8</Case><Case(21)>9</Case><Case(22)>10</Case><Case(23)>11</Case></Switch><Else/>12</If> <If(GreaterThanOrEqualTo(TopLevelParameter(218),12))>p.m.<Else/>a.m.</If> (Earth time) for all plots marked “Under Care.”

The care for plots marked “Care Halted” will resume automatically once the required conditions for maintenance have been achieved, and collection of the daily fee will resume accordingly.

■Ending Gardening Services
From the Cropland Management menu, select the plot you wish to exempt from gardening services, and choose either the “Dismiss Gardener” or “Collect Yield & End Gardening Services” subcommand.

■Collecting Yields
Gardening mammets will harvest your crops for you, but these yields will not be added to your isleventory until you collect them.

From the Cropland Management menu, use either the “Collect Yield & Resume Gardening Services” or “Collect Yield & End Gardening Services” subcommand to collect your crops, and continue or quit gardening services, respectively.

**You may entrust the care of your crops to the gardener, who will arrange for a team of green-thumbed mammets to sow, water, and gather yields in your stead.**

Speak with the produce producer after obtaining the appropriate mammet-sized tools to get started.

Please note that you will not receive island experience points when retrieving yields harvested through the gardening service.

■Entrusting Your Crops
From the Cropland Management menu, choose the plot you wish the gardener to maintain, and select the “Entrust to Gardener” subcommand.

If seeds have already been planted in the plot you select, the same type of seed will automatically be applied for further cultivation. If the plot you select is currently empty, choose which seeds you wish to cultivate.

A daily fee of seafarer's cowries will be required to maintain your crops. Maintenance will be suspended if seafarer's cowries are insufficient, your supply of seeds has been exhausted, or if yields have accumulated to the maximum of 50. Plots will be marked “Under Care” if maintenance is proceeding smoothly and “Care Halted” if maintenance has been suspended.

The fee of seafarer's cowries will be automatically collected each day at 5 p.m. (Earth time) for all plots marked “Under Care.”

The care for plots marked “Care Halted” will resume automatically once the required conditions for maintenance have been achieved, and collection of the daily fee will resume accordingly.

■Ending Gardening Services
From the Cropland Management menu, select the plot you wish to exempt from gardening services, and choose either the “Dismiss Gardener” or “Collect Yield & End Gardening Services” subcommand.

■Collecting Yields
Gardening mammets will harvest your crops for you, but these yields will not be added to your isleventory until you collect them.

From the Cropland Management menu, use either the “Collect Yield & Resume Gardening Services” or “Collect Yield & End Gardening Services” subcommand to collect your crops, and continue or quit gardening services, respectively.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Using handmade restraints, you can catch wild animals on your island to domesticate them.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
**Using handmade restraints, you can catch wild animals on your island to domesticate them.**

To attempt to capture a creature, enable Capture mode from the islekeep's index, and select the type of restraint you wish to use.

■Restraints
While all restraints are single-use items, they differ in regards to what type of animal they can catch. Animals can only be captured by using the appropriate restraints.

To attempt to capture a creature, enable Capture mode from the islekeep's index, and select the type of restraint you wish to use.

■Restraints
While all restraints are single-use items, they differ in regards to what type of animal they can catch. Animals can only be captured by using the appropriate restraints.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The animals you capture on your island will be sent to live in the pasture.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
**The animals you capture on your island will be sent to live in the pasture.**

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>You can interact with animals grazing in the pasture in various ways, including feeding them.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

To begin, enable Feed mode from the islekeep's index, then approach an animal in the pasture equipped with the feed item of your choice to give it the tasty treat it deserves.

Feeding an animal will improve its mood and sate its appetite.

■Feed
There are three different types of animal feed available:
island sweetfeed, island greenfeed, and premium island greenfeed.

Each feed can be crafted via the Sanctuary Crafting Log, and both types of greenfeed require produce yielded from the cropland.

■Food
Animals have the following four stages of appetite:
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>“Stuffed,” “Satisfied,” “Hungry,” and “Starving.”<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
An animal's appetite increases with time, and its mood worsens in parallel.

Any type of feed can satiate an animal until it is stuffed, and animals cannot be fed if already stuffed.

■Mood
Animals have the following five moods:
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>“Gleeful,” “Chipper,” “Content,” “Unhappy,” and “Hostile.”<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When an animal is starving, its mood will become hostile. Nourish animals in your care with food to improve their mood, and you may gather more leavings─among other boons!─in return.

Unlike appetite, an animal's mood is affected by the type of feed given to it.

・Island Sweetfeed
Improves an animal's mood by <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>one level.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Can improve the mood up to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>content<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

・Island Greenfeed
Improves an animal's mood by <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>two levels.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Can improve the mood up to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>chipper<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

・Premium Island Greenfeed
Improves an animal's mood by <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>two levels.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Can improve the mood up to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>gleeful<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

Speak with the creature comforter near the pasture to access the Pasture Management menu.

**You can interact with animals grazing in the pasture in various ways, including feeding them.**

To begin, enable Feed mode from the islekeep's index, then approach an animal in the pasture equipped with the feed item of your choice to give it the tasty treat it deserves.

Feeding an animal will improve its mood and sate its appetite.

■Feed
There are three different types of animal feed available:
island sweetfeed, island greenfeed, and premium island greenfeed.

Each feed can be crafted via the Sanctuary Crafting Log, and both types of greenfeed require produce yielded from the cropland.

■Food
Animals have the following four stages of appetite:
**“Stuffed,” “Satisfied,” “Hungry,” and “Starving.”**
An animal's appetite increases with time, and its mood worsens in parallel.

Any type of feed can satiate an animal until it is stuffed, and animals cannot be fed if already stuffed.

■Mood
Animals have the following five moods:
**“Gleeful,” “Chipper,” “Content,” “Unhappy,” and “Hostile.”**
When an animal is starving, its mood will become hostile. Nourish animals in your care with food to improve their mood, and you may gather more leavings─among other boons!─in return.

Unlike appetite, an animal's mood is affected by the type of feed given to it.

・Island Sweetfeed
Improves an animal's mood by **one level.** Can improve the mood up to **content**.

・Island Greenfeed
Improves an animal's mood by **two levels.** Can improve the mood up to **chipper**.

・Premium Island Greenfeed
Improves an animal's mood by **two levels.** Can improve the mood up to **gleeful**.

Speak with the creature comforter near the pasture to access the Pasture Management menu.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The workshop provides a place for mammets to create handicrafts out of the materials obtained on your island.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
**The workshop provides a place for mammets to create handicrafts out of the materials obtained on your island.**

The following elements are crucial to isleworks handicraft creation:

・Materials
The production of handicrafts requires materials that can be gathered from the wilds of your island, the crops you grow, or the animals you keep.

・Time
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The time it takes to create a given item is displayed in hours.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> In order to produce a given handicraft, a sufficient block of time must be available in the workshop's agenda.
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>※Please refer to “Agenda” for further details.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

・Value
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The selling price of each handicraft is displayed in seafarer's cowries.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> As your workshop's level increases, so too will the value of the items it produces.

・Category
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Every isleworks handicraft falls under a certain category, which in turn affects efficiency bonuses.
※Please refer to “Efficiency Bonuses” for further details.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Additional handicrafts will become available for creation as your sanctuary rank increases.

The following elements are crucial to isleworks handicraft creation:

・Materials
The production of handicrafts requires materials that can be gathered from the wilds of your island, the crops you grow, or the animals you keep.

・Time
**The time it takes to create a given item is displayed in hours.** In order to produce a given handicraft, a sufficient block of time must be available in the workshop's agenda.
**※Please refer to “Agenda” for further details.**

・Value
**The selling price of each handicraft is displayed in seafarer's cowries.** As your workshop's level increases, so too will the value of the items it produces.

・Category
**Every isleworks handicraft falls under a certain category, which in turn affects efficiency bonuses.
※Please refer to “Efficiency Bonuses” for further details.**

Additional handicrafts will become available for creation as your sanctuary rank increases.

The workshop's agenda indicates which isleworks handicrafts will be created at what time.

Speak with the tactful taskmaster near your cozy cabin to access the Isleworks Agenda interface, from which you may create, review, and edit the schedule of production.

The workshop's agenda indicates which isleworks handicrafts will be created at what time.

Speak with the tactful taskmaster near your cozy cabin to access the Isleworks Agenda interface, from which you may create, review, and edit the schedule of production.

①Pre-production
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Handicrafts in pre-production have been scheduled, but not yet created.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Production will begin at the time indicated on the agenda.

②In Production
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Handicrafts in production are currently being created.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

③Insufficient Material
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>You do not have the materials required for creating these handicrafts.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

④Shipped
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Shipped handicrafts have been completed and sold.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Setting the Agenda
To schedule handicrafts for production, select the plus button on the Isleworks Agenda interface.

①Pre-production
**Handicrafts in pre-production have been scheduled, but not yet created.** Production will begin at the time indicated on the agenda.

②In Production
**Handicrafts in production are currently being created.**

③Insufficient Material
**You do not have the materials required for creating these handicrafts.**

④Shipped
**Shipped handicrafts have been completed and sold.**

■Setting the Agenda
To schedule handicrafts for production, select the plus button on the Isleworks Agenda interface.

Handicrafts available for production will be displayed in the Set Agenda window. By selecting your desired item followed by the Schedule button, creation of the handicraft will be added to the agenda.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>You may schedule handicrafts for production even if you do not currently have the necessary materials.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> As long as sufficient materials are acquired by the time of scheduled production, the handicraft will be created automatically.

■Clearing the Agenda
Handicrafts not yet in production may be erased from the schedule.

■Rest Cycles
As a conscientious employer, you are required to allocate 2 cycles of rest per season. During rest cycles, no handicrafts can be created.

You can schedule and review rest cycles by selecting the Set Rest Cycles option in the upper right-hand corner of the Isleworks Agenda interface.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Efficiency bonuses will cause those handicrafts produced immediately after similarly categorized crafts to be produced twofold, thereby doubling earnings.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Even though double the handicrafts will be created under an efficiency bonus, no additional materials are required.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Furthermore, efficiency bonuses may be chained.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Using efficiency bonuses strategically can vastly increase your workshop's earnings.

■Limitations
Efficiency bonuses will not be applied to the same item created in sequence. Additionally, efficiency bonuses are only applicable within a given cycle, and will not carry over to the following cycle.

■Groove
When efficiency bonuses are triggered, your mammets' happy production groove will intensify. High groove scores will apply a further bonus to profits when handicrafts are shipped.

Groove is reset at the beginning of each season. Maximum groove attainable will also increase with the number of landmarks constructed on your island.

The following settings concerning the camera's placement and focal point can be adjusted:
・Tilt (Tumble)
・Distance (Dolly)
・Positioning (Track)
・Rotation (Roll)
・Lens (Zoom)

■Tilt (Tumble)
This setting affects the camera angle with respect to the focal point (your character). It can be adjusted using the right stick on a <If(Equal(PlayerParameter(75),0))>gamepad<Else/>controller</If> or by left-clicking and dragging the mouse.
※Use of the positioning (track) settings may change the portrait's focal point.

■Distance (Dolly)
Camera distance functions similarly to the zoom function in the overworld. It can be adjusted by pressing <CommandIcon(12)/> and tilting the right stick on a <If(Equal(PlayerParameter(75),0))>gamepad<Else/>controller</If>, or by scrolling with the mouse wheel while the cursor is over your portrait.

■Positioning (Track)
Positioning moves the focal point both horizontally and vertically, and can be adjusted via the left stick on a <If(Equal(PlayerParameter(75),0))>gamepad<Else/>controller</If>, or by right-clicking and dragging the mouse while the cursor is over your portrait.

■Rotation (Roll)
This slider, found below the portrait currently being edited, rotates the camera itself to adjust the direction of its focus.

■Lens (Zoom)
As opposed to the camera itself, this setting affects the camera lens's zoom to make the field of view wider or narrower. Its slider is also found below the portrait.

Choose the portrait you wish to apply the copied settings to, and select the subcommand “Import Settings.” A pop-up window will appear.

Isleworks handicrafts are artisanal goods made from your island's natural resources. Traders in Limsa Lominsa will be happy to pay a sum of seafarer's cowries in exchange for these goods, allowing you to live your dream of running an island-based small business!

In case breaking a sweat isn't your cup of tea, fear not─a team of mammets will create and ship all handicrafts for you. You need only speak to the tactful taskmaster to design the production schedule, then sit back, relax, and watch the cowries pour in.

Managing a workshop's schedule may seem daunting at first, but once you grasp the basics, handicraft production will feel as refreshing as a tropical breeze! As an example, let's try scheduling the production of an isleworks potion.

■Press Plus to Schedule
The plus button on the Isleworks Agenda screen indicates an opening in your schedule. Select this button to get started.

Managing a workshop's schedule may seem daunting at first, but once you grasp the basics, handicraft production will feel as refreshing as a tropical breeze! As an example, let's try scheduling the production of an isleworks potion.

■Press Plus to Schedule
The plus button on the Isleworks Agenda screen indicates an opening in your schedule. Select this button to get started.

After pressing the plus button, a menu of all handicrafts currently available for production will open. In keeping with our example, select “Isleworks Potion” from the list. ■Export Your Items and Reap Your Rewards!
Now that you have set an agenda, your faithful team of crafty mammets will create and export all the handicrafts you ordered while you bask in the sunshine and imbibe refreshing liquids.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>An isleworks potion takes four hours (Earth time) to produce.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Remember: those who would judge a mammet for the time it takes to produce a handicraft are not worthy of that goodly mammet.

■Managing Materials
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Materials used for the creation of a handicraft will be depleted at the start of production.

So, if you did not have enough island palm leaves or islewort when you scheduled the isleworks potion, be sure to gather two of each by the time production is scheduled to start. Don't be the Warrior of Light who made a mammet cry.

You can keep track of what materials will be expended during current or upcoming production by clicking the Review Materials button.

Mastering the intricacies of isleworks production and trade begins with a close examination of the materials available to you and the strategic implementation of efficiency bonuses.

For those islekeeps who feel like they have a firm grasp on the basics, the following tips will help you take your island industry─and earnings─to the next level.


■Popular Items Sell Even Better When Scarce
When a handicraft has High or Very High popularity, the inclination may be to produce large quantities of it, as popularity increases selling price. However, simply selling lots of a popular item will result in Overflowing supply for future cycles, which may ultimately undercut potential earnings.

By waiting to sell a popular handicraft during cycles in which its supply is Nonexistent, you can optimize your profits while keeping the markets in balance.

Demand Shift may prove a helpful indicator of how supply levels will behave in coming cycles. For example, if an Insufficient handicraft is showing a Skyrocketing demand shift, one might reasonably expect it to reach Nonexistent supply by the following cycle.


■Start the Season Short and Efficient
Levels of groove increase with the number of efficiency bonuses activated during production. Therefore, securing as many efficiency bonuses as you can at the beginning of a season allows you to benefit from the effects of maximum groove for longer.

To quickly activate efficiency bonuses, schedule applicable handicrafts that require relatively short production times. Handicrafts that take longer to produce typically fetch a higher price, so waiting to create these items until groove is high will maximize your earnings without flooding supply.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Strategically utilizing factors such as groove and paying close attention to production time will help increase the value of the handicrafts you sell.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Mastering the intricacies of isleworks production and trade begins with a close examination of the materials available to you and the strategic implementation of efficiency bonuses.

For those islekeeps who feel like they have a firm grasp on the basics, the following tips will help you take your island industry─and earnings─to the next level.


■Popular Items Sell Even Better When Scarce
When a handicraft has High or Very High popularity, the inclination may be to produce large quantities of it, as popularity increases selling price. However, simply selling lots of a popular item will result in Overflowing supply for future cycles, which may ultimately undercut potential earnings.

By waiting to sell a popular handicraft during cycles in which its supply is Nonexistent, you can optimize your profits while keeping the markets in balance.

Demand Shift may prove a helpful indicator of how supply levels will behave in coming cycles. For example, if an Insufficient handicraft is showing a Skyrocketing demand shift, one might reasonably expect it to reach Nonexistent supply by the following cycle.


■Start the Season Short and Efficient
Levels of groove increase with the number of efficiency bonuses activated during production. Therefore, securing as many efficiency bonuses as you can at the beginning of a season allows you to benefit from the effects of maximum groove for longer.

To quickly activate efficiency bonuses, schedule applicable handicrafts that require relatively short production times. Handicrafts that take longer to produce typically fetch a higher price, so waiting to create these items until groove is high will maximize your earnings without flooding supply.

**Strategically utilizing factors such as groove and paying close attention to production time will help increase the value of the handicrafts you sell.**

With an agenda in hand and a team of eager mammets at your disposal, you are free to make any and all handicrafts your heart desires. However, a very special bonus awaits those who choose their schedules cleverly.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The efficiency bonus will double the production of a given handicraft without requiring additional materials or production time.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Doubling the production of a handicraft means you will have two handicrafts to export, and basic mathematics tells us that two handicrafts will sell for twice as much as one handicraft will. So what are you waiting for? Activate those efficiency bonuses by following the steps below!

■Activating Efficiency Bonuses
All handicrafts belong to at least one item category, such as “Concoctions” or “Confections.”

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Efficiency bonuses are activated by scheduling the production of similarly categorized items in direct succession.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

For example, an isleworks potion and isleworks firesand are both classified as “Concoctions.” By scheduling production of isleworks firesand right after your isleworks potion, you will activate an efficiency bonus that creates two pinches of isleworks firesand with the same time and materials it would normally take to make one!

But you don't have to keep track of all your bonuses in that steel-trap mind of yours─the text “Efficiency Bonus!” will appear on the agenda for items that will be produced twice as a result of your clever scheduling.

With an agenda in hand and a team of eager mammets at your disposal, you are free to make any and all handicrafts your heart desires. However, a very special bonus awaits those who choose their schedules cleverly.

**The efficiency bonus will double the production of a given handicraft without requiring additional materials or production time.**

Doubling the production of a handicraft means you will have two handicrafts to export, and basic mathematics tells us that two handicrafts will sell for twice as much as one handicraft will. So what are you waiting for? Activate those efficiency bonuses by following the steps below!

■Activating Efficiency Bonuses
All handicrafts belong to at least one item category, such as “Concoctions” or “Confections.”

**Efficiency bonuses are activated by scheduling the production of similarly categorized items in direct succession.**

For example, an isleworks potion and isleworks firesand are both classified as “Concoctions.” By scheduling production of isleworks firesand right after your isleworks potion, you will activate an efficiency bonus that creates two pinches of isleworks firesand with the same time and materials it would normally take to make one!

But you don't have to keep track of all your bonuses in that steel-trap mind of yours─the text “Efficiency Bonus!” will appear on the agenda for items that will be produced twice as a result of your clever scheduling.

But wait! There's more.

What if you scheduled an isleworks potion, followed by a pinch of isleworks firesand, followed by <Emphasis>another</Emphasis> isleworks potion?

Thanks to efficiency bonuses, what might have been only three handicrafts has now been expanded to five! Think of the profits! Keep in mind both the type and order of handicrafts you add to your agenda, and you will be swimming in an abundance of seafarer's cowries in no time.

■Limitations of the Efficiency Bonus
Unfortunately, due to the peculiarities of the mammet's psyche, your tiny workers will not be nearly as excited making the same item twice in a row. While an isleworks potion/isleworks firesand combination will earn you an efficiency bonus, an order of isleworks potion/isleworks potion will get you nothing but some blinking and basic adherence to general protocol.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>In other words, only different items belonging to the same category can activate efficiency bonuses.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

When aiming for bonuses, be sure you are scheduling different handicrafts. Variety, after all, is the spice of island life.

But wait! There's more.

What if you scheduled an isleworks potion, followed by a pinch of isleworks firesand, followed by *another* isleworks potion?

Thanks to efficiency bonuses, what might have been only three handicrafts has now been expanded to five! Think of the profits! Keep in mind both the type and order of handicrafts you add to your agenda, and you will be swimming in an abundance of seafarer's cowries in no time.

■Limitations of the Efficiency Bonus
Unfortunately, due to the peculiarities of the mammet's psyche, your tiny workers will not be nearly as excited making the same item twice in a row. While an isleworks potion/isleworks firesand combination will earn you an efficiency bonus, an order of isleworks potion/isleworks potion will get you nothing but some blinking and basic adherence to general protocol.

**In other words, only different items belonging to the same category can activate efficiency bonuses.**

When aiming for bonuses, be sure you are scheduling different handicrafts. Variety, after all, is the spice of island life.

Now that you have taken your first steps down the path to profits, you may be curious as to how to increase your earnings even further than what a simple efficiency bonus might afford.

When you feel ready to take the plunge into the intricacies of the handicraft trade, access the Guide in your islekeep's index and check the section titled “The Workshop.”

But for now, perhaps continue enjoying that refreshing beverage─you've earned it!

You may enter the Palace of the Dead solo or in a party of up to four players. When speaking with the Wood Wailer expeditionary captain, you will be given the option to enter with a fixed party or a matched party via the matching system.

To create a fixed party, the party leader must speak with the Wood Wailer expeditionary captain after forming a party of up to four players. Please note that all party members must have at least one unused save slot.

Players must activate the Cairn of Passage that appears on each floor in order to progress. These stones can only be activated when all nearby enemies are defeated.

Especially powerful enemies can be found on certain floors. Players can save their progress in the Palace of the Dead upon defeating these enemies. In the event all party members are incapacitated, however, you will be removed from the Palace of the Dead and must resume from your last save point.

Players will, on occasion, obtain pomanders from treasure coffers in the Palace of the Dead. These items can be used by all party members once acquired. They have various effects such as revealing the entirety of a map or increasing the number of treasure coffers on a given floor. Effective use of these items will help ensure the situation is always to your advantage.

Pomanders that affect the map cannot be overwritten with the same item when their effect is already in play. Please note, however, that they will still be consumed upon use.

Pomanders cannot be taken outside of the deep dungeon. When in a matched party, they will be reset each time you enter the dungeon.

You may apply gear set portraits to your adventurer plate.
※You must equip the corresponding gear set to apply its portrait.

■How to Apply a Gear Set Portrait to the Adventurer Plate
1. Select the portrait you wish to apply.
2. Choose “Apply to Adventurer Plate” from the subcommand menu.
3. Select “Yes” when the confirmation window appears.

Furthermore, you may also import and apply adventurer plate portrait settings to gear set portraits.
※Only portrait settings will be imported. Gear set settings will remain unchanged.

■How to Import Your Adventurer Plate Portrait Settings
1. Select the gear set portrait to which you would like to apply the settings.
2. Choose “Import Settings from Adventurer Plate” from the subcommand menu.
3. After reviewing the confirmation window, select “Confirm.”

Standing upon a trick floor for too long will trigger a Tatami Twist, launching the unwitting loiterer into the air and inflicting damage. If your Guard is active, it will be depleted by the effort of staying upright.

Players who approach these false gates will find themselves teleported instantly to another door, far away from the expected trajectory. Trick doors only operate in one direction, so attempting to return the way you came will prove fruitless, and players may discover that passage is denied entirely whilst executing certain actions.

**■Trick Door■**
Players who approach these false gates will find themselves teleported instantly to another door, far away from the expected trajectory. Trick doors only operate in one direction, so attempting to return the way you came will prove fruitless, and players may discover that passage is denied entirely whilst executing certain actions.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Pneumatic Parades■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When a pneumatic parade begins, the clockwork onmyoji and clockwork yojimbo will join the fray. These puppets travel a predetermined path through the arena and unleash their unique attacks upon arriving in the vicinity of the centerpoint, checkpoints, and goals.

While the clockwork yojimbo will obliterate its surroundings with strong physical damage, the clockwork onmyoji quietly seeks unsuspecting players. Those unfortunate enough to meet its gaze will be greatly shrunk in size and suffer the following effects:
　・Decreased damage dealt and movement speed
　・Increased damage taken

**■Pneumatic Parades■**

While the clockwork yojimbo will obliterate its surroundings with strong physical damage, the clockwork onmyoji quietly seeks unsuspecting players. Those unfortunate enough to meet its gaze will be greatly shrunk in size and suffer the following effects:

　・Increased damage taken

During the midst of the parade, infamous soy sauce mogul Kageyama will make his grand appearance upon a stage outside the arena, and shower combatants with glimmering coin. Players who get their hands on a pile of gold will enjoy the following effect:
　・A boost to the limit gauge

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Furnishings may be placed on your island sanctuary as glamours, allowing you to further personalize your tropical hideaway.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
**Furnishings may be placed on your island sanctuary as glamours, allowing you to further personalize your tropical hideaway.**

■Placing Furnishing Glamours
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>The furnishings that appear on your island are glamours of furnishing items you own.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Select “Manage Furnishings” from the islekeep's index to place, color, or preview available furnishings.

■Placing Furnishing Glamours
**The furnishings that appear on your island are glamours of furnishing items you own.**

Select “Manage Furnishings” from the islekeep's index to place, color, or preview available furnishings.

You only need to possess one of any given furnishing item to place it as many times as you'd like on your island─within reason, of course.

There is a limit to the number of furnishing items allowed on your island at once. This number will increase as island development progresses.

Glamours may be placed in areas designated for furnishing. However, some existing furnishing items are not compatible with glamours and therefore cannot be placed anywhere on your island.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>You do not need prisms to place furnishing glamours, and furnishing items will remain in your inventory even after you have placed them as glamours. However, please note that placing a glamour will bind the furnishing item to you, preventing future trade or sale to other players.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■Registering Glamours
<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Island prisms may be used to register furnishing glamours.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Registered glamours may be placed as many times as desired via the Registered Glamours list, which can be toggled to from the Furnishing Glamours window.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>You may register up to 120 furnishing glamours.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

You can register a furnishing glamour in the following ways:
①Via the subcommand of the item while managing island furnishings
②Via the subcommand of the glamour in your Placed Glamours list
③Via the prompt when placing a glamour
④Via the prompt when removing a glamour

■Coloring Glamours
Furnishing glamours may be painted by selecting “Manage Furnishings” from the islekeep's index, followed by “Color Furnishing Glamours.”

As with regular furnishings, dye is required to change the color of furnishing glamours.

You only need to possess one of any given furnishing item to place it as many times as you'd like on your island─within reason, of course.

There is a limit to the number of furnishing items allowed on your island at once. This number will increase as island development progresses.

Glamours may be placed in areas designated for furnishing. However, some existing furnishing items are not compatible with glamours and therefore cannot be placed anywhere on your island.

**You do not need prisms to place furnishing glamours, and furnishing items will remain in your inventory even after you have placed them as glamours. However, please note that placing a glamour will bind the furnishing item to you, preventing future trade or sale to other players.**

■Registering Glamours
**Island prisms may be used to register furnishing glamours.** Registered glamours may be placed as many times as desired via the Registered Glamours list, which can be toggled to from the Furnishing Glamours window.

④Via the prompt when removing a glamour

To add a fashion accessory to your collection, use the corresponding item in your inventory. To equip your new accessory or assign it to the hotbar for later use, open the Fashion Accessories window from the Character section of the main menu.

You may stow your current accessory via the “Use” subcommand in the Fashion Accessories menu, or by selecting “Put Away” on the pet hotbar.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>※Fashion accessories are unavailable in dungeons and certain other areas. <UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

To add a fashion accessory to your collection, use the corresponding item in your inventory. To equip your new accessory or assign it to the hotbar for later use, open the Fashion Accessories window from the Character section of the main menu.

You may stow your current accessory via the “Use” subcommand in the Fashion Accessories menu, or by selecting “Put Away” on the pet hotbar.

**※Fashion accessories are unavailable in dungeons and certain other areas. **

Many types of fashion accessories can be found throughout the realm, including colorful parasols and umbrellas, stylish knapsacks, and wings of all shapes and sizes.

Some accessories come with unique actions─for example, equipping an umbrella or parasol will allow you to perform an umbrella dance.

Many types of fashion accessories can be found throughout the realm, including colorful parasols and umbrellas, stylish knapsacks, and wings of all shapes and sizes.

Some accessories come with unique actions─for example, equipping an umbrella or parasol will allow you to perform an umbrella dance.

The pet hotbar will appear when a fashion accessory is equipped, allowing you to easily change poses and perform unique actions.

By default, the pet hotbar automatically replaces a hotbar or cross hotbar. You may disable this function from the Hotbar Settings tab of the Character Configuration menu.

■Disable Automatic Hotbar Replacement
From the Character Configuration menu, select the Hotbar Settings tab. Uncheck “Automatically replace hotbar 1 when a fashion accessory is equipped.”

The pet hotbar will appear when a fashion accessory is equipped, allowing you to easily change poses and perform unique actions.

By default, the pet hotbar automatically replaces a hotbar or cross hotbar. You may disable this function from the Hotbar Settings tab of the Character Configuration menu.

■Disable Automatic Hotbar Replacement
From the Character Configuration menu, select the Hotbar Settings tab. Uncheck “Automatically replace hotbar 1 when a fashion accessory is equipped.”

Fashion accessories will remain equipped while you speak with NPCs, engage in battle, and ride mounts.

Some accessories may disappear for a short time while performing certain actions, but they will remain equipped.

For example, if a parasol is equipped, riding a mount will temporarily hide it, but the parasol will display again once you have dismounted.

Afeared of being soaked by a sudden downpour? Keep your favorite umbrella or parasol at the ready with the auto-umbrella feature!

Simply open the Fashion Accessories window under Character in the main menu, then select “Enable Auto-umbrella” from the subcommands of your preferred umbrella or parasol. The accessory you selected will be brought out the moment the rain begins, and put away once the weather clears up or you find shelter.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>※You may only set one auto-umbrella at a time.
※The auto-umbrella function is disabled in certain areas.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

A <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>gear set<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> allows you to register and save your current equipment.

Numerous gear sets can be registered to make efficient use of the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Armoury system<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>─by registering gear sets with different weapons or tools equipped to your main hand, you may switch between classes and jobs instantly without the need to manually equip each piece of gear.

If you unlock a new class or job, you may find it helpful to register a gear set for it.

A **gear set** allows you to register and save your current equipment.

Numerous gear sets can be registered to make efficient use of the **Armoury system**─by registering gear sets with different weapons or tools equipped to your main hand, you may switch between classes and jobs instantly without the need to manually equip each piece of gear.

If you unlock a new class or job, you may find it helpful to register a gear set for it.

Firstly, let's create a gear set using the items you currently have equipped.

■How to Register a Gear Set
1. Open Character from the main menu, then select the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Gear Set List button<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> on the top right.
2. A new window will be displayed. Select the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>plus button<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> found in the top left corner.
3. Your current equipment will now be registered as a new gear set.

■How to Equip a Gear Set
1. Open Gear Set List and select the desired gear set.
2. Confirm your selection with the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Equip Set button<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> at the bottom of the window.

An “E” icon next to a gear set indicates your currently equipped selection.

Firstly, let's create a gear set using the items you currently have equipped.

■How to Register a Gear Set
1. Open Character from the main menu, then select the **Gear Set List button** on the top right.
2. A new window will be displayed. Select the **plus button** found in the top left corner.
3. Your current equipment will now be registered as a new gear set.

■How to Equip a Gear Set
1. Open Gear Set List and select the desired gear set.
2. Confirm your selection with the **Equip Set button** at the bottom of the window.

An “E” icon next to a gear set indicates your currently equipped selection.

■Display Set
Display the equipment registered to the gear set in a slot-specific list.

■Preview
View your character wearing the selected gear set.

■Display Set
Display the equipment registered to the gear set in a slot-specific list.

■Preview
View your character wearing the selected gear set.

Changes to your equipment can easily be reflected to your currently selected gear set.

■How to Update Gear Sets
1. Open Character from the main menu.
2. Press the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Update Gear Set button<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> in the top right corner.

Remember to update your gear sets whenever you upgrade your equipment or make changes to materia!

Changes to your equipment can easily be reflected to your currently selected gear set.

■How to Update Gear Sets
1. Open Character from the main menu.
2. Press the **Update Gear Set button** in the top right corner.

Remember to update your gear sets whenever you upgrade your equipment or make changes to materia!

■Reassign Set Number
Directly assign a gear set number. Using an existing gear set number will result in the two sets swapping numbers.

■Move Set Up/Down
Shift a gear set to the number above or below it on the list.
※Deleted set numbers will be skipped.

Make use of these commands to assign a convenient number or order to the gear sets you use most often.

■Reassign Set Number
Directly assign a gear set number. Using an existing gear set number will result in the two sets swapping numbers.

■Move Set Up/Down
Shift a gear set to the number above or below it on the list.
※Deleted set numbers will be skipped.

Make use of these commands to assign a convenient number or order to the gear sets you use most often.

■Link to Glamour Plate
You can change the outward appearance of gear sets by linking them to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>glamour plates<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. Furthermore, by registering the same equipment as multiple gear sets, each set can be linked a different glamour plate, allowing you to switch looks to match your current mood at the touch of a button!

After linking a gear set, the glamour plate's number will be displayed to the right of the list. Please note that glamour plates can only be applied to equipment while within areas that classify as sanctuaries.

■Remove Glamour Plate Link
Links to glamour plates can be removed with the gear set subcommand. Existing links must also be removed before you will be able to assign a different glamour plate.

■Creating Glamour Plates
Read the section in this guide entitled “Customizing Ensembles” to learn more about creating glamour plates.

■Link to Glamour Plate
You can change the outward appearance of gear sets by linking them to **glamour plates**. Furthermore, by registering the same equipment as multiple gear sets, each set can be linked a different glamour plate, allowing you to switch looks to match your current mood at the touch of a button!

After linking a gear set, the glamour plate's number will be displayed to the right of the list. Please note that glamour plates can only be applied to equipment while within areas that classify as sanctuaries.

■Remove Glamour Plate Link
Links to glamour plates can be removed with the gear set subcommand. Existing links must also be removed before you will be able to assign a different glamour plate.

■Creating Glamour Plates
Read the section in this guide entitled “Customizing Ensembles” to learn more about creating glamour plates.

■Change Set Name
Your current class or job will become the default name for a newly registered set. You can use the subcommand to change this name freely, a function which comes in especially useful when creating multiple sets for the same class/job.

■Delete Set
Remove unwanted sets from the list. The numbers assigned to these sets will also be removed.

■Change Set Name

While much of the equipment in FINAL FANTASY XIV provides advantages in battle, certain pieces are worn purely for the sake of fashion. Mix and match your favorite fashion items to create a style all your own!

A special sidequest will unlock customization options allowing you to use these favored pieces to change the appearance of other equipment. Combined with the flexibility of gear sets, you control how you wish to look as you forge ahead on your adventures.

The ensemble customization guide can be accessed via the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>? button<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> found on each of these customization features, offering a detailed explanation of the various options available to create a style all your own.

While much of the equipment in FINAL FANTASY XIV provides advantages in battle, certain pieces are worn purely for the sake of fashion. Mix and match your favorite fashion items to create a style all your own!

A special sidequest will unlock customization options allowing you to use these favored pieces to change the appearance of other equipment. Combined with the flexibility of gear sets, you control how you wish to look as you forge ahead on your adventures.

The ensemble customization guide can be accessed via the **? button** found on each of these customization features, offering a detailed explanation of the various options available to create a style all your own.

Including options such as poses, expressions, camera angles, and picture frames, the portrait feature gives you the tools to create the perfect snapshot of your character.

The portrait you compose will be displayed at a variety of times, including the commencement of dungeons and awarding player commendations, as well as the start of competitive matches such as Crystalline Conflict PvP and Doman mahjong in the Gold Saucer.

To create a portrait, open Character from the main menu, and select Portraits. You can also open the portrait interface via the Edit Portrait subcommand in the gear set list.

One portrait can be saved to each gear set. The portrait linked to your currently equipped gear set will be displayed as your instant portrait.

For more information on how to edit your portraits, press the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>? button<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> found at the bottom of the Portraits interface to access the corresponding guide.

To create a portrait, open Character from the main menu, and select Portraits. You can also open the portrait interface via the Edit Portrait subcommand in the gear set list.

One portrait can be saved to each gear set. The portrait linked to your currently equipped gear set will be displayed as your instant portrait.

For more information on how to edit your portraits, press the **? button** found at the bottom of the Portraits interface to access the corresponding guide.

The <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Moogle Treasure Trove<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> is a special event held at irregular intervals. Complete objectives during the event to obtain <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>irregular tomestones<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, which can be traded to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>itinerant moogles<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> for a variety of rare items.

The **Moogle Treasure Trove** is a special event held at irregular intervals. Complete objectives during the event to obtain **irregular tomestones**, which can be traded to **itinerant moogles** for a variety of rare items.

The Moogle Treasure Trove is held for a set duration, which can be viewed from the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Mogpendium<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. From this menu, select <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Access Special Site<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> to view a detailed schedule.

An icon will appear above itinerant moogles while the event is underway.

The Moogle Treasure Trove is held for a set duration, which can be viewed from the **Mogpendium**. From this menu, select **Access Special Site** to view a detailed schedule.

An icon will appear above itinerant moogles while the event is underway.

Irregular tomestones can be obtained by completing objectives during the Moogle Treasure Trove event, and can be exchanged for various rewards.
<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>※The types of irregular tomestones available will change each time the event is held.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

The objectives for the Moogle Treasure Trove can be confirmed from the Mogpendium.
Objectives are divided into the following four categories:

1. **Standard objectives**, which can be completed repeatedly.
2. **Weekly objectives**, which can be completed once a week.
3. **Minimog challenges**, which can be completed once a week.
4. **Ultimog challenges**, which can only be completed once per event period.

Non-repeatable objectives provide greater quantities of tomestones, so prioritize them to get the items you want.

Crystalline Conflict settings can be adjusted via the Character Configuration menu. Open UI Settings, navigate to the HUD tab, and select or deselect options under the Crystalline Conflict (PvP) heading to best suit your style of play.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Progress Gauge■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Enabling “Display affiliated team on left side of Progress Gauge.” will configure the Progress Gauge to always display your current team─Astra or Umbra─on the left. Changes to this setting made during a match will be reflected upon commencement of the next match.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Map■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Enabling “Display affiliated team on left side of map.” will configure the map to always display your current team's base on the left. Changes to this setting made during a match will be reflected upon commencement of the next match.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Countdown■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Select “Display final countdown.” to show a countdown timer for the final ten seconds of the match.

Crystalline Conflict settings can be adjusted via the Character Configuration menu. Open UI Settings, navigate to the HUD tab, and select or deselect options under the Crystalline Conflict (PvP) heading to best suit your style of play.

**■Progress Gauge■**
Enabling “Display affiliated team on left side of Progress Gauge.” will configure the Progress Gauge to always display your current team─Astra or Umbra─on the left. Changes to this setting made during a match will be reflected upon commencement of the next match.

**■Map■**
Enabling “Display affiliated team on left side of map.” will configure the map to always display your current team's base on the left. Changes to this setting made during a match will be reflected upon commencement of the next match.

**■Countdown■**
Select “Display final countdown.” to show a countdown timer for the final ten seconds of the match.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Shifting Sands■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Ride the shifting sands to travel swiftly to a specific location on the field.

**■Shifting Sands■**
Ride the shifting sands to travel swiftly to a specific location on the field.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Sandpits■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The crystal line intersects with these ringlike nests at checkpoints. Clearing any such checkpoint with the tactical crystal in tow will result in one of the following denizens emerging from within:

・Red Sands Antlion
The antlion will drag all players in range deep into the nest, rendering them immobile before erupting from the sands to deal massive damage.

・Sabotender
The sabotender will unleash 100,000 Needles upon all players in range. As the needles are split between affected players, the damage dealt to each player will change accordingly.

・Acutender
This rare and curious creature will unleash Therapeutic Needles upon all players in range, granting them a barrier effect. As the needles are split between affected players, the strength of the barrier granted to each player will change accordingly.

* If the match proceeds into overtime, monsters will no longer emerge from the sandpits.

**■Sandpits■**
The crystal line intersects with these ringlike nests at checkpoints. Clearing any such checkpoint with the tactical crystal in tow will result in one of the following denizens emerging from within:

・Acutender
This rare and curious creature will unleash Therapeutic Needles upon all players in range, granting them a barrier effect. As the needles are split between affected players, the strength of the barrier granted to each player will change accordingly.

* If the match proceeds into overtime, monsters will no longer emerge from the sandpits.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Oasis■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The arena's oasis area grants players within a special effect that regenerates HP over time. The effect will remain for a time after exiting the oasis.

**■Oasis■**
The arena's oasis area grants players within a special effect that regenerates HP over time. The effect will remain for a time after exiting the oasis.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Heat Waves■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Heat waves atop the Red Sands will deal damage over time to all competitors. However, relief can be found at sandpits, from which balming geysers will erupt. Players standing in one of the small circular indicators on the ground will be soothed by sandpearl shrouds, granting the following effects:
・HP recovery.
・A boost to the limit gauge.

While the geyser is erupting, no creatures will emerge from the sandpit. Nevertheless, caution is advised, as the force of the geyser will deal damage to and knock back any who approach its center.

**■Heat Waves■**
Heat waves atop the Red Sands will deal damage over time to all competitors. However, relief can be found at sandpits, from which balming geysers will erupt. Players standing in one of the small circular indicators on the ground will be soothed by sandpearl shrouds, granting the following effects:
・HP recovery.
・A boost to the limit gauge.

While the geyser is erupting, no creatures will emerge from the sandpit. Nevertheless, caution is advised, as the force of the geyser will deal damage to and knock back any who approach its center.

Blunderville is a battle royale mode where up to 24 players vie against each other in a set of obstacle courses. Complete each of the three stages before your rivals and fight for your right to the victor's crown.

The longer you remain in the running, the more rewards you'll earn, so keep your eyes on the prize as you race to be the last one standing!

Blunderville is a battle royale mode where up to 24 players vie against each other in a set of obstacle courses. Complete each of the three stages before your rivals and fight for your right to the victor's crown.

The longer you remain in the running, the more rewards you'll earn, so keep your eyes on the prize as you race to be the last one standing!

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Registering for a Show■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
You may speak with the Blunderville registrar to participate in a show.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Classes and Jobs■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
All jobs and classes, including limited jobs, may participate in Blunderville.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Solo Registration■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Solo challengers may speak with the Blunderville registrar to participate.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Party Registration■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The party leader must speak with the Blunderville registrar to join the show with their currently formed party. Parties can be registered regardless of size.

Please note that your final rank will be calculated by personal progress rather than party progress.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Alliance Registration■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The alliance leader must speak with the Blunderville registrar to join the show with their currently formed alliance. You must have a full alliance of 24 players to participate.

**■Registering for a Show■**
You may speak with the Blunderville registrar to participate in a show.

**■Classes and Jobs■**
All jobs and classes, including limited jobs, may participate in Blunderville.

**■Solo Registration■**
Solo challengers may speak with the Blunderville registrar to participate.

**■Party Registration■**
The party leader must speak with the Blunderville registrar to join the show with their currently formed party. Parties can be registered regardless of size.

Please note that your final rank will be calculated by personal progress rather than party progress.

**■Alliance Registration■**
The alliance leader must speak with the Blunderville registrar to join the show with their currently formed alliance. You must have a full alliance of 24 players to participate.

Players who have registered for a show will be sent to wait in Lobbyville. The show will begin when 24 challengers have gathered, or after a set amount of time has passed since the first challenger entered Lobbyville.

Rewards will be adjusted based on the number of challengers in each show.

Players who have registered for a show will be sent to wait in Lobbyville. The show will begin when 24 challengers have gathered, or after a set amount of time has passed since the first challenger entered Lobbyville.

Rewards will be adjusted based on the number of challengers in each show.

Those who qualify for the next stage will enter spectator mode, where they may freely watch the remaining challengers compete. Please note that you will become invisible while spectating.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Spectating when Qualified■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Challengers who qualify for the next stage will remain in spectator mode until the next round begins.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Spectating when Eliminated■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Challengers who have been eliminated may remain in spectator mode until the end of the show. If you wish to leave spectator mode, you may do so at any time by selecting the Leave option from the Blunderville menu.

Those who qualify for the next stage will enter spectator mode, where they may freely watch the remaining challengers compete. Please note that you will become invisible while spectating.

**■Spectating when Qualified■**
Challengers who qualify for the next stage will remain in spectator mode until the next round begins.

**■Spectating when Eliminated■**
Challengers who have been eliminated may remain in spectator mode until the end of the show. If you wish to leave spectator mode, you may do so at any time by selecting the Leave option from the Blunderville menu.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■MGF■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
MGF, or Manderville Gold Saucer Fame, is awarded to all Blunderville challengers after a show has concluded. MGF is calculated based on your final rank in the show.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Exchanging MGF■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
MGF can be exchanged in Blunderville Square for exclusive rewards.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Viewing Your MGF■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
You may view the MGF currently in your possession by speaking with the MGF trader. Up to 10,000 MGF can be held at a time.

**■MGF■**
MGF, or Manderville Gold Saucer Fame, is awarded to all Blunderville challengers after a show has concluded. MGF is calculated based on your final rank in the show.

**■Exchanging MGF■**
MGF can be exchanged in Blunderville Square for exclusive rewards.

**■Viewing Your MGF■**
You may view the MGF currently in your possession by speaking with the MGF trader. Up to 10,000 MGF can be held at a time.

Glamours are a means of projecting the appearance of one piece of equipment onto another.

This is a reversible effect which can be redone any number of times.

Casting glamours requires catalysts known as <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>glamour prisms<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, with one prism being consumed per cast.

Glamours are a means of projecting the appearance of one piece of equipment onto another.

This is a reversible effect which can be redone any number of times.

Casting glamours requires catalysts known as **glamour prisms**, with one prism being consumed per cast.

To cast a glamour, select <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Character<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> in the main menu and open the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Actions & Traits<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> window, then select the Cast Glamour action under the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>General<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> tab. You can also use this action from the subcommand menu of the item you wish to glamour.

From the Cast Glamour window, choose the base item, then select the glamour you wish to cast from the list of available items.

To cast a glamour, select **Character** in the main menu and open the **Actions & Traits** window, then select the Cast Glamour action under the **General** tab. You can also use this action from the subcommand menu of the item you wish to glamour.

From the Cast Glamour window, choose the base item, then select the glamour you wish to cast from the list of available items.

Glamour prisms can be purchased from the market board, obtained from Grand Company quartermasters, or crafted using recipes learned from glamour prism recipe tomes.

Glamour prism recipe tomes can be purchased from Goberin in western Thanalan (X:12.1 Y:15.0) or Tataroga in Mor Dhona (X:22.1 Y:6.7) after completing the quest <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>“Absolutely Glamourous”<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> in western Thanalan (X:12.6 Y:14.3).

Glamour prisms can be purchased from the market board, obtained from Grand Company quartermasters, or crafted using recipes learned from glamour prism recipe tomes.

Glamour prism recipe tomes can be purchased from Goberin in western Thanalan (X:12.1 Y:15.0) or Tataroga in Mor Dhona (X:22.1 Y:6.7) after completing the quest **“Absolutely Glamourous”** in western Thanalan (X:12.6 Y:14.3).

Restoring a glamoured item to its original appearance requires the use of <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>glamour dispellers<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, which are available for purchase from the market boards or certain vendors.

From the Cast Glamour window, select the glamoured item on the left, then select Remove on the right. This action will consume a glamour dispeller.
<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>※Dyes and company crests are also removed in the process.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

**※Dyes and company crests are also removed in the process.**

You will not be able to cast a glamour if:

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>1. The base item is of a different type than that of the glamour to be cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: A gladiator's arm cannot be glamoured onto an arcanist's grimoire.
※Unaffected by item level (IL).

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>2. The base item's race or gender requirement is not compatible with that of the glamour cast.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Example: Elezen-specific gear cannot be glamoured onto Hyur-specific gear.

You will not be able to cast a glamour if:

**1. The base item is of a different type than that of the glamour to be cast.**
Example: A gladiator's arm cannot be glamoured onto an arcanist's grimoire.
※Unaffected by item level (IL).

**2. The base item's race or gender requirement is not compatible with that of the glamour cast.**
Example: Elezen-specific gear cannot be glamoured onto Hyur-specific gear.

Some glamours will only display as long as the character's race, gender, and Grand Company meet the requirements.
Example: If a race-specific glamour is applied, the effect will not be displayed.

<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>※Some relic weapons cannot be stored.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

If you wish to equip a stored item directly, you may retrieve it from the glamour dresser to restore it to its original form.

Available in inn rooms or placed as furnishings in player estates, armoires are used to store <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>artifact armor<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>seasonal event costumes<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>achievement rewards<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>bonus items<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, and other specific gear.

Available in inn rooms or placed as furnishings in player estates, armoires are used to store **artifact armor**, **seasonal event costumes**, **achievement rewards**, **bonus items**, and other specific gear.

Once an item has been stored in the armoire, select <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Apply Glamour<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> from the subcommand menu to glamour your current equipment without spending a glamour prism.

Alternatively, items in the armoire can be added to <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>glamour plates<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> without needing to store them in the glamour dresser.

Note that any dyes applied to the item will be reset. To preserve existing dyes or add new ones before applying glamours, it is recommended to store the item in the glamour dresser instead.

Once an item has been stored in the armoire, select **Apply Glamour** from the subcommand menu to glamour your current equipment without spending a glamour prism.

Facewear refers to a range of facial accessories that can add extra flourish to an ensemble. Unlike standard gear, facewear is purely decorative.

Facewear is acquired by using the corresponding item. Once acquired, facewear can be worn or removed at any time. To do so, simply select Facewear via the Character tab in the Actions & Traits menu, or click the Facewear icon to the bottom left of the character window. This will display an overview of your registered items.

Select an item of facewear to wear in the **Style** section, and customize it by choosing a **Color**.

Dyed items can be redyed and undyed any number of times.

If the circle is filled in, as shown in the center, the item has been dyed with the corresponding color.

If there are no circles, as shown on the right, the item cannot be dyed.

If there are no circles, as shown on the right, the item cannot be dyed.

Enmity is the priority system by which an enemy chooses a target. All players entered into combat with the enemy generate enmity through dealing damage or healing allies.

There are two ways to check your current enmity level:

①The Enmity List
The enmity list shows all enemies with whom you have generated enmity. The icon on the left indicates your current enmity level. The four levels are displayed below in order of greatest to least.

Enmity is the priority system by which an enemy chooses a target. All players entered into combat with the enemy generate enmity through dealing damage or healing allies.

There are two ways to check your current enmity level:

①The Enmity List
The enmity list shows all enemies with whom you have generated enmity. The icon on the left indicates your current enmity level. The four levels are displayed below in order of greatest to least.

②The Party List
The party list shows each party member's enmity level with the currently selected enemy, as indicated in the lower left corner of the class/job icon.

The player with the greatest enmity is marked with A, followed by 2, 3, and so on. The adjacent gauge shows the relative difference in enmity compared to the player with the most.

②The Party List
The party list shows each party member's enmity level with the currently selected enemy, as indicated in the lower left corner of the class/job icon.

The player with the greatest enmity is marked with A, followed by 2, 3, and so on. The adjacent gauge shows the relative difference in enmity compared to the player with the most.

During party combat, if you find that the enemies are swarming you instead of the tank, you may wish to withhold your attacks until the tank has regained enmity.

This marker indicates that a powerful area of effect attack is imminent. As a stack marker is all but impossible to survive alone, it is imperative that the targeted player stand with as many allies as possible to divide the damage evenly.

Note that there are two different types of stack marker:

■ Circle Stack

This marker indicates that a powerful area of effect attack is imminent. As a stack marker is all but impossible to survive alone, it is imperative that the targeted player stand with as many allies as possible to divide the damage evenly.

Note that there are two different types of stack marker:

■ Circle Stack

This marker warns of an incredibly powerful attack. Typically, it targets the player with the most enmity, and because it deals tremendous damage, most non-tank jobs are unlikely to survive it.

Should you be targeted, your defensive abilities can help keep you safe. For example, tanks can use Rampart to lower damage sustained as well as Reprisal to lower enemy damage output. For all other roles, check the Actions & Traits menu to see which defensive abilities your job can learn.

These markers count down to an imminent attack, and if you are caught doing a forbidden action when it reaches zero, you will be penalized with damage or an enfeeblement.

All countdown markers serve the same function regardless of enemy or duty, and over time you will learn the forbidden actions typically associated with each one.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Outfit glamours<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> are a means of projecting the appearance of specific item combinations onto an outfit that can be stored in the glamour dresser.
