■Basic Rules
Players can enter the Occult Crescent solo, in a party, or in an alliance of between nine and 48 players. It is possible to form a party even after entering the instance.

■Requirements
A <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Disciple of War or Magic level 100 or higher<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> is required to enter, and the <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>required average item level is 690<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

■Restrictions
Once inside, you are free to change to any Disciple of War or Magic that meets the requirements to enter. However, you cannot change jobs in certain circumstances, including while participating in a critical encounter.

All expeditions on the Occult Crescent are level synced, with <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>character levels adjusted to 100, and all equipment adjusted to item level 700<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

■Basic Rules
Players can enter the Occult Crescent solo, in a party, or in an alliance of between nine and 48 players. It is possible to form a party even after entering the instance.

■Requirements
A **Disciple of War or Magic level 100 or higher** is required to enter, and the **required average item level is 690**.

■Restrictions
Once inside, you are free to change to any Disciple of War or Magic that meets the requirements to enter. However, you cannot change jobs in certain circumstances, including while participating in a critical encounter.

All expeditions on the Occult Crescent are level synced, with **character levels adjusted to 100, and all equipment adjusted to item level 700**.

Progress during the expedition is represented by your <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>knowledge level<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, with all players starting at level 1.

Discrepancies between your knowledge level and that of your enemies can greatly impact the outcome of a battle. Caution is advised when engaging a foe with a knowledge level greater than your own.

Progress during the expedition is represented by your **knowledge level**, with all players starting at level 1.

Discrepancies between your knowledge level and that of your enemies can greatly impact the outcome of a battle. Caution is advised when engaging a foe with a knowledge level greater than your own.

Players can accumulate <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>knowledge<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>─akin to experience points─by defeating enemies and completing quests on the Occult Crescent.

Slaying foes with a knowledge level higher than your own will garner more knowledge. Players may choose to form parties and take on such adversaries to reap the increased knowledge reward.

Knowledge can also be earned by participating in critical encounters and FATEs across the island.

Your knowledge level and accrued knowledge will be retained between excursions─progress will not be lost when you depart the Occult Crescent.

Knowledge can also be earned by participating in critical encounters and FATEs across the island.

Your knowledge level and accrued knowledge will be retained between excursions─progress will not be lost when you depart the Occult Crescent.

Falling in battle while out on an excursion will incur a penalty. <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>If you are incapacitated and return to base camp without being revived, you will lose a portion of your knowledge.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> The amount of knowledge lost is dependent on your knowledge level. Players who leave the instance while incapacitated will also receive the aforementioned penalty.

Furthermore, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>if your knowledge level is above 5, incurring the penalty can decrease your knowledge level<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> if your knowledge falls below zero as a result.

Falling in battle while out on an excursion will incur a penalty. **If you are incapacitated and return to base camp without being revived, you will lose a portion of your knowledge.** The amount of knowledge lost is dependent on your knowledge level. Players who leave the instance while incapacitated will also receive the aforementioned penalty.

Furthermore, **if your knowledge level is above 5, incurring the penalty can decrease your knowledge level** if your knowledge falls below zero as a result.

While exploring the Occult Crescent, players can utilize additional, supplemental jobs known as <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>phantom jobs<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. While exploring the Occult Crescent, players can utilize additional, supplemental jobs known as **phantom jobs**. Selecting a phantom job bestows a **beneficial status and grants access to an array of phantom actions**.

Although only one phantom job may be selected, under most circumstances you are free to make a different selection at any time. Leveraging various phantom jobs as the need arises may be the difference between victory and defeat out in the field.

Earning sufficient phantom EXP will increase the level of your selected phantom job, granting access to a variety of <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>offensive, restorative, and supplemental phantom actions<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

Utilizing a phantom job to defeat your adversaries will garner **phantom EXP**. Earning sufficient phantom EXP will increase the level of your selected phantom job, granting access to a variety of **offensive, restorative, and supplemental phantom actions**.

Freelancers do not gain experience through traditional means. <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Instead, it levels up with every additional phantom job you master.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

On the Occult Crescent, a party is considered balanced <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>so long as all members are within three knowledge levels of one another.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
On the Occult Crescent, a party is considered balanced **so long as all members are within three knowledge levels of one another.**

Enemies defeated by a balanced party will award <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>10% more phantom experience.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Additionally, because your knowledge level is adjusted to the average level of all party members, everyone is able to take part in combat on even footing─an appealing advantage compared to exploring alone!

Enemies defeated by a balanced party will award **10% more phantom experience.**

Additionally, because your knowledge level is adjusted to the average level of all party members, everyone is able to take part in combat on even footing─an appealing advantage compared to exploring alone!

If your party is not balanced, each member will enter combat at their current knowledge level, resulting in a strength discrepancy.

To avoid this, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>the party leader may choose to toggle knowledge level sync on or off.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

When enabled, this setting adjusts the knowledge level of all members three or more levels higher than the member whose knowledge level is lowest, <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>automatically creating a balanced party.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

If your party is not balanced, each member will enter combat at their current knowledge level, resulting in a strength discrepancy.

To avoid this, **the party leader may choose to toggle knowledge level sync on or off.**

When enabled, this setting adjusts the knowledge level of all members three or more levels higher than the member whose knowledge level is lowest, **automatically creating a balanced party.**

Under certain conditions, defeating enemies on the Occult Crescent will result in <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>EXP chains.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Generally, it occurs upon defeating <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>an enemy whose knowledge level exceeds your own,<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> but the exact threshold will change depending on party size.

Solo: 2 levels higher
2 players: 3 levels higher
3 or 4 players: 4 or 5 levels higher
5 or 6 players: 6 or 7 levels higher
7 or 8 players: 7 or 8 levels higher

Under certain conditions, defeating enemies on the Occult Crescent will result in **EXP chains.**

Generally, it occurs upon defeating **an enemy whose knowledge level exceeds your own,** but the exact threshold will change depending on party size.

Solo: 2 levels higher
2 players: 3 levels higher
3 or 4 players: 4 or 5 levels higher
5 or 6 players: 6 or 7 levels higher
7 or 8 players: 7 or 8 levels higher

The more high-level enemies defeated within a set time period, the larger the chain will grow; <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>the bigger the level difference, the faster the growth rate.<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow> Form a party with your comrades and reap greater rewards!

The more high-level enemies defeated within a set time period, the larger the chain will grow; **the bigger the level difference, the faster the growth rate.** Form a party with your comrades and reap greater rewards!

Critical encounters are large-scale battles wherein up to 72 players band together to take on powerful opponents. Prove yourself victorious and you'll gain considerable knowledge, among other rewards.

Unlike enemies in the wild, foes fought in critical encounters are <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>unaffected by knowledge level,<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> so all players who join the fray will do so on even ground.

Even better, a uniquely powerful enhancing effect will be applied to those who win the battle under set conditions!

Critical encounters are large-scale battles wherein up to 72 players band together to take on powerful opponents. Prove yourself victorious and you'll gain considerable knowledge, among other rewards.

To take part in a critical encounter, **you need only reach its location on the map.** This may necessitate travel through areas infested with dangerous enemies, so have care.

The Forked Towers are the greatest challenge to be found on the Occult Crescent.

Make full use of your phantom jobs and work together with up to 48 total players to overcome powerful foes and tricky battle mechanics!

As the battle progresses, the weaponry displacement system will come into effect. While activated, three types of neutral weaponry may be deployed around the Heliodrome:

　1. Interceptor Drones
　2. Interceptor Command Systems
　3. Interceptor Nodes
As the battle progresses, the weaponry displacement system will come into effect. While activated, three types of neutral weaponry may be deployed around the Heliodrome:

　1. Interceptor Drones
　2. Interceptor Command Systems
　3. Interceptor Nodes

Interceptor drones are small enemies that engage surrounding players with automatic cannons before self-detonating after an allotted time has passed. Defeating them will distribute tactical points proportional to damage contribution and raise the rating of the party that contributed the most.

When interceptor drones are deployed, a golden interceptor drone α may appear. Defeating these rare enemies will result in greater rewards than regular interceptor drones. Both types of enemies will keep respawning as long as the weaponry displacement system is in effect.

Interceptor drones are small enemies that engage surrounding players with automatic cannons before self-detonating after an allotted time has passed. Defeating them will distribute tactical points proportional to damage contribution and raise the rating of the party that contributed the most.

When interceptor drones are deployed, a golden interceptor drone α may appear. Defeating these rare enemies will result in greater rewards than regular interceptor drones. Both types of enemies will keep respawning as long as the weaponry displacement system is in effect.

Interceptor nodes are large enemies that indiscriminately target surrounding players with a variety of attacks.
　・Ground Interference:
　　A large area-of-effect attack.
　・Heavy Interference:
　　A stack marker attack.
　・Targeted Interference:
　　A honing attack.

Every time this enemy's HP is reduced to zero, it will gain one point. When it has accrued five points, it will begin to flash, indicating that it may be defeated. Teams will accrue tactical points according to damage contribution each time the interceptor node's HP is reduced to zero.

Personnel displacement panels are one-way teleporters installed throughout the field for personal use. They may be active or inactive depending on the state of battle.
　・Active:
　　Thirty seconds before the weaponry displacement system activates.
　・Inactive:
　　When the weaponry displacement system deactivates and at the
　　start of battle.

Cosmic Exploration is <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>an initiative wherein Disciples of the Hand and Land join forces to achieve interstellar travel.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Cosmic Exploration is **an initiative wherein Disciples of the Hand and Land join forces to achieve interstellar travel.**

■The Basics
For this project to achieve liftoff, Searchingway has need of your artisanal skills. Objectives can be completed solo or in groups.

As the initiative progresses, your surrounds will be expanded with new facilities and areas to explore. <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Work together with the denizens of your Home World and watch your efforts pay off in real time!<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

■The Basics
For this project to achieve liftoff, Searchingway has need of your artisanal skills. Objectives can be completed solo or in groups.

As the initiative progresses, your surrounds will be expanded with new facilities and areas to explore. **Work together with the denizens of your Home World and watch your efforts pay off in real time!**

To ensure the success of the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, contributors are asked to undertake a variety of different activities.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Stellar Missions<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Stellar missions enlist Disciples of the Hand and Land to leverage their specialist skills and provide materials and crafted items to help further the initiative.

You can confirm rewards and requirements of currently available missions via the Stellar Missions section of the exotablet.

To ensure the success of the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, contributors are asked to undertake a variety of different activities.

**■Stellar Missions**
Stellar missions enlist Disciples of the Hand and Land to leverage their specialist skills and provide materials and crafted items to help further the initiative.

You can confirm rewards and requirements of currently available missions via the Stellar Missions section of the exotablet.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Mech Ops & Red Alerts<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Mech op directives and red alerts are issued without warning to contributors in the field.

The former sees participants pilot mechanized walkers known as vacuum suits to undertake a variety of missions. Due to the technical nature of this work, these missions offer special rewards unobtainable through other activities.

■Cosmic Crafting Log
Exclusive to cosmic exploration, this crafting log contains recipes specific to Disciple of the Hand stellar missions.

Choose a recipe, then select the Synthesize button to begin cosmic crafting in much the same fashion as ordinary crafting.

Exclusive to cosmic exploration, this crafting log contains recipes specific to Disciple of the Hand stellar missions.

Choose a recipe, then select the Synthesize button to begin cosmic crafting in much the same fashion as ordinary crafting.

■Stellar Reduction
A type of aetherial reduction performed upon materials acquired through Disciple of the Land stellar missions.

■Abandon
Select the Abandon button to forfeit an incomplete mission.

■Ratings
Upon completing a mission, you will be awarded a bronze, silver, or gold star rating according to the level of your success. This, in turn, affects the mission's reward payout as follows:

Bronze: No Bonus
Silver: 4x Bonus
Gold: 5x Bonus
Furthermore, you will gain a fixed amount of experience regardless of tier.

Any mission completed with a bronze or silver star rating will henceforth have a checkmark beside its name in the mission selection list, whereas missions completed with a gold star rating will have a special symbol instead.

By assisting in the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, player contributions are recorded and tallied over regular intervals. These accumulated scores are used to rank players in the Starward Standings.

Players with the highest score at the end of any given ranking period are designated **Star Contributor**.

Ranking periods will last twenty-four hours (Earth time), from 6 p.m. (Earth time) to 6 p.m. (Earth time) the following day. Once the Star Contributors have been selected, a twenty-four hour interim will follow before the next ranking period begins.
**※In the event of server maintenance during a ranking period, players with the highest score in the available time before or after maintenance will be designated Star Contributor.**

Player scores can be increased by undertaking stellar missions, or participating in mech ops and cosmic exploration projects.

After completion of the aforementioned activities, a system message will display in the log window noting the size of your contribution.

To learn more about these tallying periods or your present score, **speak with Scanningway at the current exploration area's base camp.**

Please note that **Starward Standings will only be recorded in the current exploration area**.

Players selected as Star Contributor will receive special accolades that last for fourteen days (Earth time) from the time they are chosen.

While Star Contributors cannot be selected again during this fourteen day period, should they have the highest score on the fourteenth day, they can maintain their status as Star Contributor without interruption.

Whenever these hazards result in major obstacles to the initiative, the Moongate Hub will issue a <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>red alert<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. Heed the call to arms, and work together with your comrades to resolve the crisis!
Whenever these hazards result in major obstacles to the initiative, the Moongate Hub will issue a **red alert**. Heed the call to arms, and work together with your comrades to resolve the crisis!
During an active red alert, you may aid in its resolution by undertaking <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>critical missions<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.
During an active red alert, you may aid in its resolution by undertaking **critical missions**.

Each progress bar represents a different ongoing problem. Your objective is to ensure that <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>both are filled within the allotted time<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. If successful, all contributors shall receive bonus rewards at the conclusion of the red alert.

Each progress bar represents a different ongoing problem. Your objective is to ensure that **both are filled within the allotted time**. If successful, all contributors shall receive bonus rewards at the conclusion of the red alert.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>So long as the timer has yet to expire, you are free to undertake critical missions, even if the progress bars are already full.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> Your reward will not be impacted.

For the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, there's no such thing as too many supplies, so don't hesitate to contribute if you have the time!

**So long as the timer has yet to expire, you are free to undertake critical missions, even if the progress bars are already full.** Your reward will not be impacted.

For the Cosmic Exploration Initiative, there's no such thing as too many supplies, so don't hesitate to contribute if you have the time!

Critical missions may only be undertaken while a red alert is active. Upon its conclusion, you will be unable to complete the mission.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>Additionally, once the timer falls below one minute, you will be unable to accept a new mission, as you will not have sufficient time to complete it.<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Critical missions may only be undertaken while a red alert is active. Upon its conclusion, you will be unable to complete the mission.

**Additionally, once the timer falls below one minute, you will be unable to accept a new mission, as you will not have sufficient time to complete it.**

Cosmocredits are a type of currency earned through contributions to the Cosmic Exploration Initiative. At the Credit Exchange, they can be used to purchase a variety of supplies and even exclusive wares!

■How to Earn Cosmocredits
Cosmocredits are primarily earned through the completion of stellar missions, but are also awarded through stellar successes and other avenues. Some tasks, such as construction projects, are more lucrative than others!

Cosmocredits are a type of currency earned through contributions to the Cosmic Exploration Initiative. At the Credit Exchange, they can be used to purchase a variety of supplies and even exclusive wares!

Though objectives, rating milestones, and other details may vary, each mission generally falls under one of the following categories:

■Stocking Specified Items
Utilize your abilities to collect a specific item. Upon reaching the requisite quantity, the mission is complete.

Disciples of the Hand may earn greater compensation by submitting high-quality items, whereas fishers will be richly rewarded for large-sized fish. You can review the requisite milestones on the item help window.

■Stocking Specified Item Types
Utilize your abilities to collect items of specified types. The more types of items obtained, the higher your rating upon mission completion. Each new type of item obtained increases your rating by 1,000 points.

■Race against Time
For these missions, the objective must be completed within the time limit. The faster you finish, the better your rating, increasing by 10 points for every remaining second.

■Increasing Collectability
Utilize your abilities to collect items with high collectability. The higher the collectability, the higher your rating upon completion. You can review the requisite milestones on the item help window.

The exotablet is <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>a HUD feature exclusive to cosmic exploration zones<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> from which you may review important information pertaining to every aspect of the initiative.
The exotablet is **a HUD feature exclusive to cosmic exploration zones** from which you may review important information pertaining to every aspect of the initiative.

■Main Window
From this window, you have access to each facet of exploration, enabling you to accept stellar missions and check the overall progress of the initiative.

■Cosmocredits
Cosmocredits are a type of formless currency issued by the initiative and accepted at any interstellar base camp. The amount in your possession is displayed on your exotablet.

■Sector Credits
Each cosmic exploration zone uses a unique formless currency. As with cosmocredits, the amount of credits applicable to your current location is displayed on your exotablet.

Players participating in the Cosmic Exploration Initiative are subject to a number of rules and effects.

■Traveling to Exploration Zones
Players may speak with Drivingway in Bestways Burrow (X:21.9 Y:13.2) to gain passage to cosmic exploration zones. Players may also travel directly to the zone currently under development via the aetheryte in Bestways Burrow.

■Stellar Return
Stellar Return can be used anywhere within cosmic exploration zones. Upon use, players are transported to the rainbow crystal located at the zone's base of operations.

■Stellar Sprint
Within cosmic exploration zones, the rainbow crystal converts a crafter or gatherer's Sprint action into Stellar Sprint, boosting movement speed and effect duration.

In the beginning, you are only permitted to undertake class D stellar missions. You will gradually unlock more difficult tasks upon reaching the requisite conditions, which can be found below the mission selection list.

Unlocking tasks in any given area will unlock stellar missions for all exploration areas.

There are a few special rules and restrictions associated with stellar missions:

■Quest Sync
The difficulty of crafting and gathering missions, as well as the amount of experience earned, will be adjusted to your current level at the time of commencement.

■GP Recovery
Upon completing gathering missions, you will recover GP equal to the total amount spent during the mission. Furthermore, the recast timer of recovery items such as cordials will be reset.

■Duty Actions
Some stellar missions allow for the use of a unique duty action. This information is listed in the mission selection window.

Certain gatherer missions also have class-specific rank requirements.

■Successive Gathering Chains (Miner and Botanist Only)
Achieve as many successful chains as possible at a single gathering point. The higher the chain, the higher your rating.
Chain #1: 50
Chain #2: 50
Chain #3: 50
Chain #4: 150
Chain #5+: 200

■Gatherer's Boon (Miner and Botanist Only)
Trigger Gatherer's Boon as many times as possible. Rating is increased by 200 each time the effect is triggered.

■Big Fish (Fisher Only)
Your rating is determined by the size of fish caught. Some missions will evaluate your single biggest catch, while others measure the average size of your entire haul. In either case, your rating is tallied using the formula “fish size x 10.”

You may enter Pilgrim's Traverse solo or in a party of up to four players. When speaking with Vanthau, you will be given the option to enter with a fixed party or a matched party via the matching system.

To create a fixed party, the party leader must speak with Vanthau after forming a party of up to four players. Please note that all party members must have at least one unused save slot.

You must be a Disciple of War or Magic of level 91 or above to participate.

Players who enter from traverse stone 1 will start at level 91 regardless of their current class or job level. Players who enter from traverse stones 21, 31, 51, or 71 will start at level 100.

While an independent leveling system is in place within the dungeon, experience points that affect current class or job level will be rewarded based on progress.

It is possible to reset your progression in Pilgrim's Traverse, allowing you to re-enter the chapels from previous traverse stones.

If entering from stone 1, your level will be reset to 91. If entering from stones 21, 31, 51, or 71, your level will be reset to 100. Although area progression and character level will be reset, aetherpool arm and gear strength will remain unchanged. Please note, however, that data cannot be restored once deleted.

Illumed aetherpool gear can be enhanced by accessing silver coffers. These coffers are found randomly in each area and after defeating certain enemies. Please note that aetherpool gear possessed of significant strength may, in some cases, fail to resonate properly with silver coffers, resulting in failed enhancement.

The maximum strength of illumed aetherpool gear may be restricted depending on your class or job level and current dungeon progression. This can be confirmed when viewing the Character Info window.

Aetherpool gear enhancements will not carry over from one deep dungeon to another.

Players will, on occasion, obtain pomanders from treasure coffers in Pilgrim's Traverse. These items can be used by all party members once acquired. They have various effects such as revealing the entirety of a map or increasing the number of treasure coffers in a given area. Effective use of these items will help ensure the situation is always to your advantage.

Pomanders that affect the map cannot be overwritten with the same item when their effect is already in play. Please note, however, that they will still be consumed upon use.

Pomanders cannot be taken outside of the deep dungeon. When in a matched party, they will be reset each time you enter the dungeon.

The Final Verse (Quantum) is an independent duty that pits a four-player party against an enhanced incarnation of Eminent Grief. At least 15 sacramental offerings must be collected in order to challenge this duty.

Once a party leader has acquired sufficient offerings, they must form a four-player party with Disciples of War or Magic of level 100 or above and speak with Vanthau to invoke the Final Verse (Quantum). After selecting at least 15 offerings, Eminent Grief may be summoned and the duty commenced.

Please note that phoenix down cannot be used during the Final Verse (Quantum).

Please note that phoenix down cannot be used during the Final Verse (Quantum).

The editing of strategy boards is carried out primarily through the use of three menus:

The Objects List window, where you can choose icons and symbols to add to the strategy board.
The Object Layers window, where you can perform various functions such as hiding, locking, and copying objects.
The Object Parameters window, where you can make finer adjustments such as changing the size, rotation, and transparency of objects.
※By locking these windows, they will remain open when viewing the main editing window.

When viewing the main window, you can also toggle gridlines, gridline snapping, and object snapping.

※Exchanging codes via chat is not advised, as they may be affected by player chat filters. Furthermore, depending on the contents of a strategy board, the resulting share code may exceed the character limit for chat messages.

Real-time sharing allows you to modify a strategy board together with party members.

While all players will be able to view the strategy board, only the player who initiated real-time sharing can make direct edits.

Please note that real-time sharing will be suspended during combat or when players leave or join your party. However, any changes made to the strategy board will be saved automatically to the Shared List, where you can resume editing at a later time.

Because real-time edits are overwritten periodically, you may wish to create a copy to preserve the original strategy board.

Players may obtain pieces of the Blessed Hoard by completing challenge log objectives for Pilgrim's Traverse, or by clearing the two duties unlocked over the course of the dungeon.

Just as with the Accursed Hoard, pieces of the Blessed Hoard must be appraised by Tyr Marn in Wolekdorf to ascertain their true value.

Pilgrim's Traverse differs from previous deep dungeons in a variety of ways. The changes you can expect to find are outlined below, using the Palace of the Dead as a contrasting example.

<UIForeground>F20224</UIForeground>■Floors<UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
The Palace of the Dead has a total of two hundred floors, whereas Pilgrim's Traverse has one hundred areas available for exploration.

<UIForeground>F20224</UIForeground>■Entry Points<UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
You may re-enter the dungeon from waypoints should your party KO or if you reset your progress. These entry points become available after clearing certain areas and completing quests unlocked by dungeon progression.

In the Palace of the Dead, players can only re-enter from floors 1 or 51. Pilgrim's Traverse offers entry points from stones 1, 21, 31, 51, and 71.

<UIForeground>F20224</UIForeground>■Matched Parties<UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
All floors after floor 100 of the Palace of the Dead are designed strictly as a challenge for players and only accessible to fixed parties. However, Pilgrim's Traverse allows players to challenge comparable areas, beginning with stone 31, with matched parties. Please note that from stone 91 onwards, the matching system cannot be used to add members.

<UIForeground>F20224</UIForeground>■Independent Duties<UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Two independent duties can be unlocked by proceeding through Pilgrim's Traverse. Tabs with more information on each will be added to this menu when they are unlocked.

Pilgrim's Traverse differs from previous deep dungeons in a variety of ways. The changes you can expect to find are outlined below, using the Palace of the Dead as a contrasting example.

**■Floors
The Palace of the Dead has a total of two hundred floors, whereas Pilgrim's Traverse has one hundred areas available for exploration.

**■Entry Points
You may re-enter the dungeon from waypoints should your party KO or if you reset your progress. These entry points become available after clearing certain areas and completing quests unlocked by dungeon progression.

In the Palace of the Dead, players can only re-enter from floors 1 or 51. Pilgrim's Traverse offers entry points from stones 1, 21, 31, 51, and 71.

**■Matched Parties
All floors after floor 100 of the Palace of the Dead are designed strictly as a challenge for players and only accessible to fixed parties. However, Pilgrim's Traverse allows players to challenge comparable areas, beginning with stone 31, with matched parties. Please note that from stone 91 onwards, the matching system cannot be used to add members.

**■Independent Duties
Two independent duties can be unlocked by proceeding through Pilgrim's Traverse. Tabs with more information on each will be added to this menu when they are unlocked.

Some items and features found within this dungeon are unique to Pilgrim's Traverse.

<UIForeground>F20224</UIForeground>■Special Items<UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
Alongside pomanders, treasure coffers in Pilgrim's Traverse may contain juniper incense, which can be burned to call the faerie king. Check the Special Items tab for more information.

<UIForeground>F20224</UIForeground>■Special Features<UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
In Pilgrim's Traverse, you may encounter votive candelabras, which may be lit to trigger effects in the subsequent area. Check the Votive Candelabras tab for more information.

<UIForeground>F20224</UIForeground>■Challenge Log Rewards<UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Alongside pieces of the Accursed Hoard, Pilgrim's Traverse offers a variety of rewards.

**■Challenge Log Rewards
Entries for Pilgrim's Traverse have been added to the deep dungeon category of the challenge log. Completing them will earn players special rewards.

The challenge log may be checked via the main menu or by speaking with Vanthau.

**■The Blessed Hoard
Players can collect pieces of the Blessed Hoard by completing challenge log objectives or clearing related duties. Check the Blessed Hoard tab for more information.

■基本情報
スプリグ・クリーニングは、ソロでのみ遊べるコンテンツです。
コンテンツの制限時間は3分です。

ゲーム中は、専用アクションを駆使して魔物を刈り、
より多くのスコアを獲得することが目的となります。

■報酬
ゲーム中に獲得したスコアの量に応じて、
「スペシャル・トリコエッグ」を入手することできます。

スペシャル・トリコエッグは、
ミィ・ケット野外音楽堂の「エッグドリーマー」に渡すことで、
装備品やアイテムと交換できます。

■Crafting and Gathering Restrictions
During a stellar mission, crafting and gathering will not award experience, and spiritbond will accrue at a different rate.

■Fishing Restrictions
At the end of each stellar mission, your Angler's Art stacks will be reset, regardless of how many you have accrued.

■Ground Support Bonuses
Ground supporters who make sufficient contributions to a mech op will receive bonus credits for a set number of subsequent missions, regardless of objective. These bonuses are as follows:

Cosmocredits: +20
Sector Credits: +10

For the duration of the effect, an arrow symbol will be displayed on the Initiate button on the mission selection window.

Your <UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>gold count<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> refers to a special bonus that accrues as high-level missions are completed in succession.

■Activation Conditions
To start a gold count, consecutively attain gold stars in provisional or basic missions of class A or higher. Changing classes will not break this streak.

Upon attaining another gold star after accruing 5 golds, you will achieve Super Star status and your gold count will reset to zero.

Each cosmic exploration field has a separate gold count which will be retained even if you leave the area.

■Bonus Effect
An active gold count will yield increased rewards for stellar missions, with Super Star status offering the highest bonus. Gold count and Super Star bonuses affect all credit types, dronebits, and contributions to the Starward Standings. You can confirm your current gold count via the mission selection list.

Your **gold count** refers to a special bonus that accrues as high-level missions are completed in succession.

■Activation Conditions
To start a gold count, consecutively attain gold stars in class A basic missions, including those designated as EX or EX+, or provisional missions. Changing classes will not break this streak.

Upon attaining another gold star after accruing 5 golds, you will achieve Super Star status and your gold count will reset to zero.

Each cosmic exploration field has a separate gold count which will be retained even if you leave the area.

■Bonus Effect
An active gold count will yield increased rewards for stellar missions, with Super Star status offering the highest bonus. Gold count and Super Star bonuses affect all credit types, dronebits, and contributions to the Starward Standings. You can confirm your current gold count via the mission selection list.

■Reset Conditions
The following actions will break a gold count streak:
・Initiating a class B, C, or D mission.
・Completing a mission with a rating below Gold Star.
・Abandoning a mission (unless the mission was automatically abandoned due to your mech ops pilot application having been selected).

Conversely, the following actions will not break a gold count streak:
・Taking part in mech ops.
・Taking part in cosmic exploration projects.
・Initiating and/or completing critical missions.

Artifact Search
Overview

Where there are ruins, there are treasures just begging to be found!

To begin the **artifact search**, you'll need to launch a reconnaissance drone by using a drone module, the type of which differs between stars.

■How to Obtain Drone Modules
Within cosmic exploration zones that contain ruins, whenever your gold count is activated through the consecutive completion of class A basic missions and/or provisional missions, you will receive dronebits alongside the other rewards. (Please refer to the Gold Count section of the exotablet for more information.)

Dronebits may be traded to Kaede at the local rainbow crystal in exchange for drone modules.
How to Use Drone Modules
Using a drone module will launch a reconnaissance drone and initiate its artifact scanning subroutines. The locations of discovered artifacts will be marked on your map for your convenience.
Once you arrive at the marked location, interact with the artifact to conduct analysis.

Successful analysis will produce an ancient record, completing the artifact search. The ancient record may then be submitted to Kaede at the local rainbow crystal in exchange for a variety of rewards.

In Worqor Chirteh (Triumph), victory goes to the first alliance to reach the specified tactical rating, which is accumulated by:
　1. Defeating enemy players
　2. Claiming triumphs

In the event that none of the three alliances reaches the specified tactical rating within the time limit, victory will be awarded to the alliance with the highest tactical rating.

Across the field of battle, there are points of tactical importance known as triumphs. By laying claim to these locations, players can increase their team's tactical rating over time.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Triumph States■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
In the course of battle, triumphs will assume one of four states, in the following order:

1. Inactive
When inactive, a triumph cannot be claimed. All triumphs are inactive at the onset of battle, but as time passes, they will randomly begin activating.

Across the field of battle, there are points of tactical importance known as triumphs. By laying claim to these locations, players can increase their team's tactical rating over time.

**■Triumph States■**
In the course of battle, triumphs will assume one of four states, in the following order:

1. Inactive
When inactive, a triumph cannot be claimed. All triumphs are inactive at the onset of battle, but as time passes, they will randomly begin activating.

2. Activating
When a triumph begins activating, a symbol will appear above it indicating the time remaining before it becomes unclaimed. The symbol will gradually take shape until it becomes complete, rendering the triumph unclaimed.

2. Activating
When a triumph begins activating, a symbol will appear above it indicating the time remaining before it becomes unclaimed. The symbol will gradually take shape until it becomes complete, rendering the triumph unclaimed.

3. Unclaimed
When a triumph is unclaimed, several fields of triumph will appear around it. Once a single team secures all fields, they will claim the triumph for themselves.

3. Unclaimed
When a triumph is unclaimed, several fields of triumph will appear around it. Once a single team secures all fields, they will claim the triumph for themselves.

4. Claimed
When claimed, a triumph gradually increases a team's tactical rating. A triumph that has been claimed cannot be seized by the opposition; however, after a certain amount of time has elapsed, it will revert to being inactive. When it does so, a triumph in a different location will become unclaimed.

4. Claimed
When claimed, a triumph gradually increases a team's tactical rating. A triumph that has been claimed cannot be seized by the opposition; however, after a certain amount of time has elapsed, it will revert to being inactive. When it does so, a triumph in a different location will become unclaimed.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Triumph Rank■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>
When a triumph begins activating, it will randomly be assigned a rank: B, A, or S, as indicated by the corresponding shape. While higher-ranked triumphs are rarer, they increase a team's tactical rating at a faster rate.

**■Triumph Rank■**
When a triumph begins activating, it will randomly be assigned a rank: B, A, or S, as indicated by the corresponding shape. While higher-ranked triumphs are rarer, they increase a team's tactical rating at a faster rate.

<UIForeground>F201FA</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201FB</UIGlow>■Changes during Battle■<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>

Several fields of triumph will appear around an unclaimed triumph. Players can secure a field of triumph by remaining within the area for a set period of time. The number of players within the field does not affect the time required.

The higher the rank of the triumph, the more fields will appear.

Once a field of triumph has been secured, the opposition may attempt to seize it for themselves. Even though another team may have secured the field, the battle is far from over!

You may use your illumed aetherpool weapon outside of Pilgrim's Traverse after clearing the area marked by stone 30 and completing the subsequent quest.

However, you must first forge a sufficient number of illumed aetherpool grips by speaking with Ose Wyd in Wolekdorf. Forging one grip will reduce the strength of your illumed aetherpool arm and armor by 10.

Once you have acquired enough illumed aetherpool grips, you may obtain a weapon of the First Light by speaking with Ose Wyd and selecting “Request to keep your aetherpool gear.”

Once you clear the area marked by stone 100 and enhance your illumed aetherpool arm to +60 or above, sacramental weapons will also become available.
